facts.title: Every @expo/ui subtree needs a <Host> wrapper
facts.fact: Host is the RN→SwiftUI/Compose boundary. Flexbox/Yoga applies to Host itself; inside it, layout is Column/Row (VStack/HStack), not flex styles.
facts.code:
import { Host, Column, Text } from '@expo/ui';

<Host style={{ flex: 1 }}>
  <Column spacing={12}>
    <Text>No Yoga in here - use Column/Row</Text>
  </Column>
</Host>

facts.title: Import paths: universal root, /swift-ui, /jetpack-compose
facts.fact: Root = universal components (iOS + Android, plain RN views on web). `@expo/ui/swift-ui` and `@expo/ui/jetpack-compose` are platform supersets; modifiers live at `<path>/modifiers`. Eight `community/*` drop-ins also ship.

facts.title: @expo/ui ignores className - style it with modifiers
facts.fact: @expo/ui components ignore NativeWind's `className`. SwiftUI views take an ordered `modifiers` array; universal views take a narrow `style` subset.

facts.title: The iOS switch is Toggle, and its props are not RN's
facts.fact: swift-ui has no `Switch` - it exports `Toggle` (`isOn`/`onIsOnChange`). The root universal `Switch` keeps RN's `value`/`onValueChange`.

facts.title: SwiftUI Picker has no options or variant prop
facts.fact: Options are children tagged with the `tag()` modifier; `selection`/`onSelectionChange` carry that tag, and segmented is `pickerStyle('segmented')`.

facts.title: @expo/ui/community/* replaces 8 community libraries
facts.fact: API-compatible drop-ins backed by SwiftUI/Compose: bottom-sheet (@gorhom), slider, datetime-picker, picker, segmented-control, masked-view, menu, pager-view. Swap the import path, drop the dependency.

facts.title: Host sizes like a View; matchContents is mount-only
facts.fact: An unstyled `<Host>` can collapse to zero and render nothing. `matchContents` sizes it to its content; it and `ignoreSafeArea` apply only at mount.

facts.title: A Button with custom children needs buttonStyle('plain')
facts.fact: Otherwise SwiftUI tints the whole child tree blue. Expo's own Settings-app example wraps its custom row in `buttonStyle('plain')` for this reason.

facts.title: useNativeState keeps a control's state off the JS thread
facts.fact: `useNativeState` holds state in the native UI runtime; a `Sync*` control bound to it (SwiftUI's `SyncToggle`) updates without a JS round-trip.

facts.title: Use Icon.select(), not a plain {ios, android} object
facts.fact: The @expo/ui Babel plugin rewrites `Icon.select` to the target platform's value at build time; an inline `{ios, android}` object ships both assets.

facts.title: distribution defaults to "store", not "internal"
facts.fact: A build profile with no `distribution` is store-signed, so you cannot sideload it or install it on a device outside TestFlight/Play. Set `"distribution": "internal"` on preview and development profiles.

facts.title: Omitting `environment` makes EAS guess your env
facts.fact: With no `environment` set, EAS infers it: `production` if distribution is store, `development` if developmentClient is true, else `preview`.

facts.title: Secret env vars break `eas update` and local builds
facts.fact: A `secret` var can't be read outside EAS servers — no `eas update`, no `--local` build, no app.config.js. `sensitive` is masked in logs but readable. Neither protects EXPO_PUBLIC values: those are inlined into the bundle.

facts.title: `eas env:pull` writes .env.local, not .env
facts.fact: Defaults to `.env.local` (change with `--path`). Secret vars can't be pulled — they land as commented placeholders for you to fill in by hand.
facts.code:
eas env:pull --environment development
# writes .env.local (override: --path)
# secrets -> # NAME=***** (secret)
facts.source: eas-cli 20.3.0: build/commands/env/pull.js — flag default '.env.local'; `# ${name}=***** (secret)` placeholder + skippedSecretVariables log

facts.title: `extends` deep-merges env, and platform blocks win
facts.fact: `env` maps merge key-by-key up an `extends` chain rather than being replaced; an `ios`/`android` block overrides the same key at the profile root.
facts.source: eas-cli 20.3.0: node_modules/@expo/eas-json/build/build/resolver.js — mergeProfiles() spreads base.env into update.env; platform block merged over profile root

facts.title: appVersionSource: remote ignores app.json build nums
facts.fact: With `remote`, EAS owns versionCode/buildNumber; values in app config are ignored and never written back. Seed with `eas build:version:set`.

facts.title: `eas build --local` ignores most of eas.json
facts.fact: `eas build --local` skips the `node`, `yarn`, `image`, `cocoapods`, `fastlane` and `ndk` fields entirely, does no caching, rejects `--platform all`, and cannot read secret env vars — set those in your shell instead.

facts.title: `--auto-submit` submits the build when it finishes
facts.fact: It picks the submit profile whose name matches the build profile. Use `--auto-submit-with-profile <name>` to point at a different one.
facts.code:
eas build -p ios --profile production \
  --auto-submit

facts.title: .easignore fully replaces .gitignore for uploads
facts.fact: Once a .easignore exists, EAS ignores .gitignore entirely — it does not merge them, so copy your .gitignore contents in or you'll upload build output and other junk. A leading `!` force-includes an untracked file.

facts.title: Publishing to a branch does not create the channel
facts.fact: A build polls its channel. If the channel was never created server-side, the update request 404s and the app silently keeps running bundled code.
facts.code:
eas channel:create preview
eas update --branch preview \
  --environment preview -m "fix"
eas channel:view preview
facts.source: https://docs.expo.dev/eas-update/debug/ (Verify the channel / Map channel to branch)

facts.title: An OTA update can never change native code
facts.fact: Updates swap the JS bundle and assets only. A new native module, permission, bundle identifier, or splash config needs a new binary. runtimeVersion is the guard that keeps a mismatched update off an old build.

facts.title: appVersion is the default runtimeVersion policy
facts.fact: Three policies at SDK 57: appVersion, nativeVersion, fingerprint. eas update:configure writes appVersion, so that is the default you get.

facts.title: The Updates API is inert in dev — test a Release build
facts.fact: checkForUpdateAsync, fetchUpdateAsync and reloadAsync all reject in Expo Go and in dev builds. Only a release build exercises real update behavior.
facts.code:
npx expo run:ios --configuration Release
npx expo run:android --variant release

facts.title: A downloaded update runs on the NEXT cold boot
facts.fact: With the default fallbackToCacheTimeout of 0, launch N downloads in the background and launch N+1 runs it — and only a true cold boot counts, not a background/foreground. So never ship first-launch-critical values by OTA.

facts.title: useUpdates(): reload when isUpdatePending flips true
facts.fact: The hook also gives currentlyRunning, isUpdateAvailable, isChecking, isDownloading, downloadProgress, checkError and downloadError.

facts.title: checkForUpdateAsync returns a three-way union
facts.fact: isAvailable = a new update; isRollBackToEmbedded = a rollback directive; otherwise `reason` says why nothing came back, e.g. updatePreviouslyFailed.

facts.title: checkAutomatically has four values, not two
facts.fact: updates.checkAutomatically accepts ON_LOAD (default), WIFI_ONLY, ON_ERROR_RECOVERY and NEVER. Set NEVER to kill the startup check and drive checks yourself from JS.

facts.title: eas update --environment ignores your local .env
facts.fact: With --environment, EXPO_PUBLIC_* values come from EAS server-side env vars only. A key that exists only in your .env ships blank in the OTA bundle.
facts.code:
eas env:create --environment production \
  --name EXPO_PUBLIC_TMDB_KEY \
  --visibility plaintext --value abc123
eas update --environment production

facts.title: Two kinds of rollback: republish, or back to embedded
facts.fact: eas update:rollback is interactive: either re-publish a known-good update, or send a roll-back-to-embedded directive so clients run the bundle baked into the binary. Publishing again supersedes the rollback for everyone.

facts.title: Error recovery only catches crashes within 10s of render
facts.fact: A fatal JS error more than 10s after first render is not caught at all. Before the first view renders, expo-updates can mark the update failed and roll back; after it renders it only fixes forward, then rethrows and crashes.

facts.title: Report isEmergencyLaunch to your error tracker
facts.fact: It is true when the binary had to fall back to the embedded bundle after an update failed to initialize; emergencyLaunchReason carries the message.

facts.title: expo-status-bar dropped backgroundColor and translucent
facts.fact: SDK 56 removed backgroundColor, translucent and networkActivityIndicatorVisible. Only style, hidden, animated and hideTransitionAnimation remain.

facts.title: Linking.createURL() needs expo.scheme in app.json
facts.fact: createURL builds a deep link from expo.scheme in app.json — no scheme, nothing to build. Expo Go emits exp://…/--/path, so auth needs a dev build.

facts.title: OAuth needs openAuthSessionAsync, not openBrowserAsync
facts.fact: openBrowserAsync only tells you the modal closed. openAuthSessionAsync uses ASWebAuthenticationSession and resolves with the redirect URL.

facts.title: Call preventAutoHideAsync() in module scope, not an effect
facts.fact: Splash auto-hides as soon as the app is ready, so a call from inside a component can land late. Put preventAutoHideAsync at module scope, unawaited.

facts.title: Embed fonts with the expo-font plugin, not useFonts
facts.fact: The config plugin links font files into the native project — available at first frame, no useFonts gate or runtime loadAsync.

facts.title: Gate GlassView with isGlassEffectAPIAvailable()
facts.fact: GlassView renders a plain View on Android and pre-iOS-26. Some iOS 26 betas ship without the API and crash, so the runtime check is not optional.

facts.title: SymbolView is not iOS-only: pass { ios, android } names
facts.fact: A bare string name is an SF Symbol — off iOS it renders nothing but your fallback. An object maps per platform; Android draws a Material Symbol.

facts.title: SystemUI paints the root view behind your React tree
facts.fact: SystemUI.setBackgroundColorAsync sets the native root view background — what shows through during navigation transitions and overscroll. No NativeWind class reaches it; call it once at module scope.

facts.title: Constants.expoConfig is how you read app.json at runtime
facts.fact: Constants.expoConfig is the resolved app config for both embedded and OTA manifests. Use executionEnvironment, not the deprecated appOwnership.

facts.title: Tokens go in expo-secure-store, never AsyncStorage
facts.fact: Not installed — `npx expo install expo-secure-store` for TMDB session ids. It encrypts via Keychain / Keystore; AsyncStorage is plaintext.

facts.title: Do not import from @react-navigation/* anymore
facts.fact: Expo Router vendors its own React Navigation fork; importing the external packages is unsupported. Repoint imports to expo-router entry points.

facts.title: Parenthesized folders are groups, not URL segments
facts.fact: A (tabs) folder applies a shared _layout but adds no URL segment. Name the group explicitly — /(feed)/profile — to target its copy of a shared route.
facts.code:
app/(tabs)/index.tsx    ->  /
app/(tabs)/search.tsx   ->  /search
app/(auth)/sign-in.tsx  ->  /sign-in

facts.title: useGlobalSearchParams re-renders background screens
facts.fact: useGlobalSearchParams fires on every URL change, so screens still mounted behind the current one re-render with the new params. Default to useLocalSearchParams: it only updates while its own route is focused.

facts.title: Link asChild forwards onPress to its child
facts.fact: <Link> renders a Text by default. asChild hands href/onPress to the first child instead — that child must accept onPress (Pressable, a custom card).
facts.code:
import { Link } from 'expo-router';
import { Pressable, Text } from 'react-native';

<Link href="/movie/278" asChild>
  <Pressable>
    <Text>Details</Text>
  </Pressable>
</Link>

facts.title: Render <Stack.Screen> inside a screen to set options
facts.fact: It is not only a layout-file declaration: rendered with no `name` from inside a screen body, it sets that route's options from fetched data or state.

facts.title: Modals are a presentation option, not a component
facts.fact: A modal route is an ordinary file in app/. Declare it in the parent Stack with presentation: 'modal' (also transparentModal, formSheet, card).
facts.code:
<Stack>
  <Stack.Screen name="index" />
  <Stack.Screen
    name="modal"
    options={{ presentation: 'modal' }}
  />
</Stack>

facts.title: Tabs from the root entry is deprecated in 57.0.4
facts.fact: The root re-export of Tabs is @deprecated — import it from the js-tabs entry. NativeTabs, the real system tab bar, is a separate alpha entry point.

facts.title: router.dismissTo replaces when the target isn't in history
facts.fact: dismiss(n) pops n screens, dismissAll() pops to top, dismissTo(href) pops until that href — and if it is not in the stack, it replaces the current screen instead of failing.

facts.title: Stack.Protected is the auth gate — no redirect code
facts.fact: A false guard makes those screens unreachable. Flipping a guard true→false while the user is on one boots them out and erases its history entries.
facts.code:
<Stack>
  <Stack.Protected guard={!!session}>
    <Stack.Screen name="(app)" />
  </Stack.Protected>
  <Stack.Protected guard={!session}>
    <Stack.Screen name="sign-in" />
  </Stack.Protected>
</Stack>

facts.title: unstable_settings.anchor sets a layout's initial route
facts.fact: Export it from a _layout to pin the route underneath — so a deep-linked modal has a screen behind it. `anchor` wins over legacy `initialRouteName`.

facts.title: Route types are generated, not checked in
facts.fact: With experiments.typedRoutes (already on in app.json), Expo CLI writes the route union to .expo/types/router.d.ts when the dev server starts. Both it and expo-env.d.ts are gitignored, so CI must generate them before tsc.

facts.title: Pass the route path as a generic for typed params
facts.fact: Both search-param hooks take the route path as their first generic, so params are typed from the filename. A second generic types query params.

facts.title: useFocusEffect takes exactly one memoized callback
facts.fact: Runs on every focus, cleans up on blur (not unmount) — the hook for refetch-on-return. Wrap the callback in useCallback; there is no deps array.

facts.title: useSegments returns filenames, not resolved values
facts.fact: Segments are the raw file path — group folders included — so /movie/278 yields '[id]', not '278'. Use usePathname() for the resolved URL.

facts.title: presentation: 'formSheet' gives you a native bottom sheet
facts.fact: No sheet library needed. sheetAllowedDetents takes ascending fractions of screen height, or 'fitToContents'. Android caps out at 3 detents.
facts.code:
<Stack.Screen
  name="filters"
  options={{
    presentation: 'formSheet',
    sheetAllowedDetents: [0.5, 1],
    sheetGrabberVisible: true,
  }}
/>

facts.title: Link.Preview gives iOS peek-and-pop for free
facts.fact: Wrap the body in Link.Trigger, add Link.Preview (iOS only). Previews need push — `replace` breaks them — and a Preview without a Trigger throws.
facts.code:
<Link href="/movie/278">
  <Link.Trigger>Details</Link.Trigger>
  <Link.Preview />
</Link>

facts.title: Prefetch a route before the user taps it
facts.fact: <Link prefetch> preloads the target while the current screen is focused; router.prefetch(href) is the imperative form. Warms the detail screen early.
facts.code:
<Link href="/movie/278" prefetch>Details</Link>

// or, before the user taps:
router.prefetch('/movie/278');

facts.title: dangerouslySingular collapses duplicate detail screens
facts.fact: Related-movie loops can stack /movie/278 twice. dangerouslySingular dedupes by route name + resolved params; the old `getId` prop is deprecated.
facts.code:
<Stack.Screen
  name="movie/[id]"
  dangerouslySingular
/>

// or per navigation:
router.push('/movie/278',
  { dangerouslySingular: true });

facts.title: Screen-view analytics: unstable_navigationEvents
facts.fact: pageFocused/pageBlurred/pagePreloaded carry pathname, params, segments. Off by default — call enable() at module scope, not in an effect.

facts.title: useScrollToTop wires tab-press to a FlashList ref
facts.fact: Tapping the already-active tab scrolls the list to top. It fires only on tabPress, while focused, and only on that tab's first screen.

facts.title: headerSearchBarOptions renders a native search bar
facts.fact: No TextInput needed. onChangeText hands you a native event, not a string. The list below needs contentInsetAdjustmentBehavior='automatic'.
facts.code:
<Stack.Screen
  options={{
    headerSearchBarOptions: {
      placeholder: 'Search movies',
      onChangeText: (e) =>
        setQuery(e.nativeEvent.text),
    },
  }}
/>

facts.title: Every route already sits in a Suspense boundary
facts.fact: useSuspenseQuery needs no boundary. But the default fallback renders null in production — export SuspenseFallback from a _layout to override it.

facts.title: npx expo prebuild wipes android/ and ios/ by default now
facts.fact: In SDK 57 prebuild clears and regenerates the native folders on every run — pass --no-clean to patch them in place instead. Anything you hand-edited in ios/ or android/ is gone unless it lives in a config plugin.

facts.title: EXPO_PUBLIC_ vars ship in plaintext inside your binary
facts.fact: Only vars prefixed EXPO_PUBLIC_ are exposed to app code, and they are inlined as string literals into the JS bundle. Anyone can extract them from a shipped .ipa/.apk, so they are config, never secrets.

facts.title: Env inlining is literal: destructuring process.env fails
facts.fact: Babel inlines only literal `process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_X` member accesses. A destructured or computed key is untouched — undefined in a release build.

facts.title: .env precedence, and why your shell var always wins
facts.fact: Expo CLI loads these per NODE_ENV, highest first. A key already set in your shell always wins — a .env file never overwrites it.
facts.code:
.env.production.local   # highest priority
.env.local              # skipped if NODE_ENV=test
.env.production
.env                    # lowest priority

facts.title: expo/fetch, not RN's fetch, is the global fetch
facts.fact: The expo entry installs a WinterTC-compliant native fetch over globalThis.fetch (plus URL, TextDecoder, structuredClone). If a network edge case bites, set EXPO_PUBLIC_USE_RN_FETCH=1 to restore RN's XHR fetch.

facts.title: Config plugin mods run at prebuild, never at runtime
facts.fact: Plugin functions run every time the app config is read; only their mods — which rewrite the native files — run at `expo prebuild`. Nothing runs at runtime, so plugins have zero effect in Expo Go: native config needs a dev build.

facts.title: expo install pins SDK-verified versions, not latest
facts.fact: Versions come from Expo's SDK 57 versions endpoint (bundledNativeModules.json when offline), so you get an SDK-verified release. `pnpm add` does not.
facts.code:
npx expo install expo-secure-store
npx expo install --check  # list mismatched deps
npx expo install --fix    # repin them to SDK 57

facts.title: npx expo-doctor is the SDK-compatibility gate
facts.fact: It checks installed dependency versions, app config and native project state against the installed SDK. The React Native Directory check is opt-in via EXPO_DOCTOR_ENABLE_DIRECTORY_CHECK=1.

facts.title: Native build settings belong in expo-build-properties
facts.fact: The config plugin for Gradle/Xcode knobs. Being a plugin, a change lands only after a prebuild plus a fresh native build — never via OTA.

facts.title: process.env.EXPO_OS beats Platform.OS in plain TS files
facts.fact: babel-preset-expo replaces it with the target platform at build time, so pure-TS modules like @app/config can branch without importing react-native.

facts.title: expo config --type introspect previews prebuild's output
facts.fact: Runs your plugins' mods in memory and prints the Info.plist/AndroidManifest they produce — writing nothing. Check a plugin before you prebuild.
facts.code:
npx expo config --type introspect  # native mods
npx expo config --type public      # OTA manifest
npx expo config --type prebuild --json

facts.title: Pre-warm Chrome Custom Tabs before the TMDB auth hop
facts.fact: warmUpAsync binds the Custom Tabs service so openAuthSessionAsync opens without a cold start. It resolves to {} on iOS, so no guard is needed.

facts.title: SplashScreen.setOptions: the fade flag is iOS-only
facts.fact: Call it before hideAsync. `duration` (default 400ms) works on both platforms; Android always alpha-fades and ignores `fade` — only iOS reads it.

facts.title: 'use no memo' opts one component out of React Compiler
facts.fact: reactCompiler is on in app.json. It compiles only your code — never node_modules — and the escape hatch is a directive in the function body.

facts.title: source takes a bare string; sizing prop is contentFit
facts.fact: No {uri} wrapper needed, and the resize prop is contentFit (CSS object-fit), default 'cover'. RN's resizeMode still parses but is deprecated.

facts.title: placeholderContentFit defaults to scale-down, not cover
facts.fact: A blurhash placeholder is laid out with 'scale-down' while the real image uses 'cover' - the swap visibly jumps unless you match them.
facts.code:
<Image
  source={uri}
  placeholder={{ blurhash }}
  placeholderContentFit="cover"
  contentFit="cover"
  transition={300}
/>

facts.title: recyclingKey is mandatory inside FlashList
facts.fact: Recycled cells keep the previous cell's bitmap until the new one loads. Changing recyclingKey blanks the view (or shows the placeholder) first.

facts.title: cachePolicy defaults to 'disk' - memory is not cached
facts.fact: Default 'disk' re-decodes from disk on every mount. Use 'memory-disk' for images you render many times (list posters, avatars); 'memory' alone is purged aggressively under pressure.

facts.title: transition={ms} is shorthand for a cross-dissolve
facts.fact: A number is the duration of a 'cross-dissolve'. The object form adds timing ('ease-in-out' default) and effect (flip-from-*, curl-*), but Android only ever honors cross-dissolve.

facts.title: Image.prefetch warms the cache, memory-disk by default
facts.fact: Static method; takes a URL or array and resolves false if any one image fails. The second arg is a cachePolicy string or an options object.

facts.title: useImage returns null until loaded - cap it with maxWidth
facts.fact: It hands back an ImageRef (width/height/scale) you pass straight to source. Without maxWidth/maxHeight, large images can OOM-crash the app.

facts.title: Import ImageBackground from expo-image, not react-native
facts.fact: expo-image ships its own: a View wrapping an absolutely-filled Image plus children. `style` sizes the container, `imageStyle` styles the image.

facts.title: onLoad tells you whether the image came from cache
facts.fact: The event carries cacheType ('none' | 'disk' | 'memory') plus the decoded width/height - the cheapest way to check a cachePolicy is working.

facts.title: allowDownscaling is on by default, but not for none/fill
facts.fact: Images are downscaled to the container to save memory; turning it off buys quality at a real perf cost. It never applies when contentFit is 'none' or 'fill' - a 4K backdrop then stays full-res in RAM.

facts.title: Array sources need width/height or the first one wins
facts.fact: Native compares each source's width×height to the container box and picks the closest. Omit them and every source scores 0 — the first one wins.
facts.code:
<Image
  style={{ width: 120, height: 180 }}
  source={[
    { uri: w342, width: 342, height: 513 },
    { uri: w780, width: 780, height: 1170 },
  ]}
/>

facts.title: configureCache is iOS-only; size limits default to 0
facts.fact: maxDiskSize, maxMemoryCost and maxMemoryCount each default to 0 — no limit. Android's module has no configureCache, so an unguarded call throws.

facts.title: getCachePathAsync finds a cached poster's file on disk
facts.fact: Pass the source URL — or your source.cacheKey, if you set one — and it resolves to the on-disk file path, or null when the image isn't cached. Use it to hand a poster to a share sheet or file API. Android and iOS only.

facts.title: FlashList v2 is estimate-free — no estimatedItemSize
facts.fact: v2 removed estimatedItemSize, estimatedListSize and estimatedFirstItemOffset. They're gone from FlashListProps, so passing one is now a TS error.

facts.title: Pad with contentContainerStyle, never with style
facts.fact: In v2 `style` targets FlashList's parent container — padding there distorts child sizing. Put padding and list insets on contentContainerStyle.
facts.code:
<FlashList
  data={movies}
  renderItem={renderItem}
  contentContainerStyle={{ padding: 16 }}
  ItemSeparatorComponent={() => (
    <View className="h-3" />
  )}
/>

facts.title: useState in a list item survives cell recycling
facts.fact: Cells are reused for other items, so a plain useState keeps the previous item's value. useRecyclingState resets it whenever its deps change.

facts.title: getItemType gives each item shape its own recycle pool
facts.fact: Without getItemType every cell shares one recycle pool, so a header can recycle into a movie card. Return a stable type per item shape; keep it cheap.
facts.code:
<FlashList
  data={rows}
  getItemType={(item) => item.kind}
  renderItem={({ item }) =>
    item.kind === "header"
      ? <SectionHeader t={item.title} />
      : <MovieCard movie={item} />
  }
/>

facts.title: Type the ref as FlashListRef<T>, not FlashList
facts.fact: FlashList is a function component; the ref type is a separate export, FlashListRef<T>. Its scrollToIndex returns a Promise that resolves on arrival.

facts.title: maintainVisibleContentPosition is ON by default in v2
facts.fact: Prepending items no longer jumps the scroll position — v2 enables maintainVisibleContentPosition by default; pass { disabled: true } to opt out. Note v2 has no `inverted` prop at all.

facts.title: Use useMappingHelper when .map()-ing inside an item
facts.fact: In a recycled cell, keying a mapped list by content (g.id) remounts those children on reuse. getMappingKey keys by index inside a FlashList instead.

facts.title: FlashList's ViewToken has a key, and a nullable index
facts.fact: FlashList exports its own ViewToken<T>: { item, key, index: number | null, isViewable, timestamp }. Guard the null index. Setting both itemVisiblePercentThreshold and viewAreaCoveragePercentThreshold throws.

facts.title: useLayoutState when item state changes the item's size
facts.fact: Plain useState resizes the cell a frame late (FlashList catches it via onLayout). useLayoutState triggers the layout pass in the same commit.

facts.title: overrideItemLayout sets span only — mutate it in place
facts.fact: Only `span` is read off the layout object; v2 ignores the size estimates it used to take. Mutate the object in place — a returned value is discarded.
facts.code:
<FlashList
  numColumns={3}
  overrideItemLayout={(layout, item) => {
    layout.span = item.featured ? 3 : 1;
  }}
  data={movies}
  renderItem={renderItem}
/>

facts.title: Cells are memoized — external state needs extraData
facts.fact: ViewHolder is React.memo'd on item, extraData and renderItem identity. A memoized renderItem closing over watchlist state renders stale rows.
facts.code:
<FlashList
  data={movies}
  extraData={savedIds}
  renderItem={({ item, extraData }) => (
    <Row m={item} saved={extraData.has(item.id)} />
  )}
/>

facts.title: stickyHeaderIndices re-renders the row as a pinned copy
facts.fact: The pinned copy calls renderItem again with target 'StickyHeader'. Give it an opaque background or rows show through. Throws on horizontal lists.
facts.code:
<FlashList
  stickyHeaderIndices={[0, 12]}
  renderItem={({ item, target }) => (
    <Row
      item={item}
      pinned={target === "StickyHeader"}
    />
  )}
/>

facts.title: Never nest a vertical FlashList in a ScrollView
facts.fact: 'Exceeded max renders without commit' means duplicate keyExtractor keys — or a vertical list nested in a ScrollView. Use ListHeaderComponent.
facts.code:
<FlashList
  data={recommendations}
  ListHeaderComponent={<DetailHeader m={movie} />}
  renderItem={renderItem}
/>

facts.title: Wrap the app in GestureHandlerRootView or Android breaks
facts.fact: On Android it mounts a native root view; elsewhere a plain View. Without it GestureDetector throws in dev; in release Android gestures never fire.

facts.title: Compose with Gesture.Pan(), mount with GestureDetector
facts.fact: The 2.x API is a chainable builder plus one wrapper component. <PanGestureHandler> and friends still exist, but they're the legacy API.

facts.title: Gesture callbacks are worklets — hop threads for state
facts.fact: The Babel plugin workletizes onStart/onUpdate/onEnd onto the UI thread, so touching React state or the router needs a hop back via scheduleOnRN.

facts.title: RNGH's Pressable can join gesture relations; RN's can't
facts.fact: When a button sits inside a gesture, import Pressable from react-native-gesture-handler, not react-native: only RNGH's takes simultaneousWithExternalGesture, requireExternalGestureToFail and blocksExternalGesture.

facts.title: Exclusive ranks by order; Race is first-to-activate
facts.fact: Gesture.Race(a, b): whichever activates first cancels the others. Gesture.Simultaneous: all may be active at once. Gesture.Exclusive(a, b): a has priority — put the double tap first so the single tap waits for it to fail.

facts.title: Use onChange for deltas; taps have no onUpdate at all
facts.fact: Pan/Pinch/Rotation/Hover are continuous: onUpdate plus onChange, which adds a delta since the last event (changeX/changeY on Pan, scaleChange on Pinch). Tap and LongPress are discrete: only onBegin/onStart/onEnd/onFinalize.

facts.title: Pan inside a scrollable needs activeOffsetX + failOffsetY
facts.fact: Without offsets the Pan steals the touch and the list stops scrolling. Gate horizontal activation, bail on vertical movement, block the list.

facts.title: Swipeable is deprecated — import ReanimatedSwipeable
facts.fact: The old Animated-based Swipeable is @deprecated. The replacement ships at a subpath and passes render props shared values plus a methods object.
facts.code:
import Swipeable from
'react-native-gesture-handler/ReanimatedSwipeable';

<Swipeable
  renderRightActions={(prog, drag, methods) => (
    <Delete onPress={methods.close} />
  )}>
  <Row />
</Swipeable>

facts.title: Tap and LongPress cancel outside the view; Pan does not
facts.fact: TapGesture and LongPressGesture call shouldCancelWhenOutside(true) in their constructors — slide off the view and they fail. Pan defaults to false and keeps tracking past the bounds. hitSlop resizes the trigger area.

facts.title: Aliasing the Gesture import silently kills workletization
facts.fact: The Babel plugin matches the literal identifier `Gesture` in the chain. Alias it and callbacks are never workletized — they run on the JS thread.

facts.title: One non-worklet callback drops the gesture to JS
facts.fact: All-or-nothing per gesture: if one callback isn't a worklet, all of them run on the JS thread. Only inline arrows get auto-workletized — inline them.
facts.source: react-native-gesture-handler/src/handlers/gestures/gesture.ts (get shouldUseReanimated: runOnJS !== true && !handlers.isWorklet.includes(false)); GestureDetector/utils.ts checkGestureCallbacksForWorklets (none-are-worklets -> console.warn; mixed -> console.error). Babel auto-workletization of inline chain callbacks: react-native-worklets 0.10 gestureHandlerAutoworkletization

facts.title: GestureDetector needs one child that keeps its native view
facts.fact: It clones the single child with collapsable={false}; a custom component swallows that prop and Fabric may flatten the view away. Two children throw.
facts.code:
<GestureDetector gesture={pan}>
  <View collapsable={false}>
    <MovieCard movie={movie} />
  </View>
</GestureDetector>
facts.source: react-native-gesture-handler/src/handlers/gestures/GestureDetector/Wrap.tsx (Children.only throw + cloneElement with collapsable: false) and useViewRefHandler.ts (isViewFlatteningDisabled dev error)

facts.title: Never share one gesture instance across detectors
facts.fact: initialize() re-stamps handlerTag on the instance, so a module-scope gesture reused by every row leaves earlier rows on a dead tag — one per row.
facts.source: react-native-gesture-handler/src/handlers/gestures/gesture.ts initialize() (handlerTag = getNextHandlerTag()), called per attach in GestureDetector/attachHandlers.ts; GestureDetector/index.tsx JSDoc Remarks: "Using the same instance of a gesture across multiple Gesture Detectors is not possible"

facts.title: Gluestack v5 copies components into your repo
facts.fact: v5 is own-your-code: `npx gluestack-ui@latest add button` writes src/components/ui/button/index.tsx into your repo. @gluestack-ui/core only ships headless factories (createButton, createToast) — you own the styled file.

facts.title: Button children must be ButtonText, never a raw string
facts.fact: Button wraps Pressable; a raw string child throws "Text strings must be rendered within a <Text> component". Compose from ButtonText / ButtonIcon.

facts.title: GluestackUIProvider's mode prop sets the color scheme
facts.fact: It is generated into your repo (src/components/ui/gluestack-ui-provider): a View wrapping OverlayProvider + ToastProvider whose mode="light"|"dark"|"system" calls Appearance.setColorScheme. v4's vars() config is gone.

facts.title: Gluestack tokens: RGB triples wired up by @theme inline
facts.fact: Tokens are shadcn-style semantic names (primary, destructive…) stored as space-separated RGB triples and exposed as Tailwind colors via @theme inline.

facts.title: tva() is where a component's variants actually live
facts.fact: Components style themselves via tva (tailwind-variants). Retuning a variant means editing that object in your own file — no theme override API.

facts.title: useToast is created by createToastHook in your toast file
facts.fact: useToast comes from your copied toast file, not a package; ToastProvider sits inside GluestackUIProvider. duration: null never auto-dismisses.

facts.title: core 5.0.15 ships bottomsheet, calendar and a date picker
facts.fact: The installed core exposes creators for ~33 components, including bottomsheet, calendar, date-time-picker, tabs, actionsheet and chat-ai — check there before pulling in a separate sheet or calendar library.

facts.title: parentVariants lets child parts inherit parent variants
facts.fact: gluestack's tva adds `parentVariants`, absent upstream: the parent publishes its variants via a scoped context and children style off them.

facts.title: Modal and Actionsheet don't use an RN Modal by default
facts.fact: Overlay defaults useRNModal:false and portals into OverlayProvider instead of a native modal. avoidKeyboard is off by default too.
facts.code:
<Modal
  isOpen={isOpen}
  onClose={onClose}
  useRNModal
  avoidKeyboard
>
  <ModalBackdrop />
  <ModalContent>{children}</ModalContent>
</Modal>

facts.title: createIcon needs Root: Svg — PrimitiveIcon swallows color
facts.fact: createIcon clones paths with fill='currentColor', which only Svg's `color` resolves; Root: PrimitiveIcon maps color onto stroke, so fills stay black.

facts.title: useDebouncedState lags — don't bind it to a TextInput
facts.fact: @gluestack-ui/utils/hooks ships useDebouncedState. Its returned value lags `wait` ms, so it can't drive a TextInput — keep raw text in a useState.

facts.title: A stable toast id dedupes a retry storm into one toast
facts.fact: setToast drops any existing toast with the same id at that placement before adding it, so repeated show()s replace instead of stacking up.

facts.title: Expo SDK 57 auto-detects your pnpm workspace root
facts.fact: getDefaultConfig walks up to the workspace root (pnpm-workspace.yaml) and fills watchFolders + resolver.nodeModulesPaths itself — the hand-copied monorepo block most configs still carry is redundant.

facts.title: Never set disableHierarchicalLookup with pnpm
facts.fact: Metro defaults it to false; keep it there. Under pnpm a package's deps live in .pnpm/<pkg>/node_modules, reachable only by walking up from the importing file. Set it true and that walk dies — bundling fails on transitive deps.

facts.title: Every import needs a dep in the app's own package.json
facts.fact: pnpm's isolated linker does not hoist — the root node_modules holds only lint/format tooling (lefthook, prettier). If apps/mobile imports it, install it there (pnpm add --filter mobile <pkg>); a transitive dep won't resolve.

facts.title: expo start -c clears transforms, not resolutions
facts.fact: Expo's transform cache is a FileStore in os.tmpdir()/metro-cache; -c, --clear and --reset-cache are the same flag. Resolutions are never cached, so resolver edits apply on a plain restart — Babel/transformer edits need -c.

facts.title: withNativewind must wrap the Metro config last
facts.fact: It swaps transformerPath and appends a resolveRequest that chains whatever resolver is already on the config — so set yours first, then wrap.
facts.code:
config.resolver.resolveRequest = myResolver;
module.exports = withNativewind(config);

facts.title: assetExts and sourceExts must stay disjoint
facts.fact: Expo's defaults list heic/avif/db as assets and ts/tsx/css/cjs as source, then filter overlap. Push new binary types onto assetExts — never both.
facts.code:
const config = getDefaultConfig(__dirname);
config.resolver.assetExts.push("lottie");

facts.title: Workspace subpath imports need an exports entry
facts.fact: Metro 0.84 defaults unstable_enablePackageExports to true. `@app/config/defaults` resolves only because the package declares that subpath.

facts.title: Overriding getTransformOptions drops Expo's defaults
facts.fact: Expo ships experimentalImportSupport: true, inlineRequires: false. Replace getTransformOptions and you must re-declare both or lose import support.

facts.title: pnpm 11 refuses versions published in the last 24 hours
facts.fact: minimumReleaseAge: 1440 makes pnpm skip releases under 24h old, so pnpm add silently picks an older version. expo/react-native/* are exempt.
facts.code:
pnpm --config.minimumReleaseAge=0 \
  --filter mobile add @sentry/react-native
facts.source: pnpm-workspace.yaml (minimumReleaseAge / minimumReleaseAgeExclude); pnpm 11.9.0 `pnpm config get minimumReleaseAge`; https://pnpm.io/settings

facts.title: allowBuilds gates postinstall scripts (pnpm 11 rename)
facts.fact: pnpm runs no install scripts unless allowBuilds names the dep (v11 renamed onlyBuiltDependencies). Add a native dep, run pnpm approve-builds.
facts.code:
allowBuilds:
  unrs-resolver: true
  lefthook: true
facts.source: pnpm-workspace.yaml; `pnpm approve-builds --help` and `pnpm config get allowBuilds` (pnpm 11.9.0); https://pnpm.io/settings (allowBuilds replaces v10 onlyBuiltDependencies)

facts.title: @app/config ships raw TS — the barrel is the bundle edge
facts.fact: packages/config has no build step — Metro bundles src/index.ts raw. The barrel is the bundle boundary, so build-time-only generate.ts stays off it.
facts.source: packages/config/package.json (main/types: src/index.ts, no build script); packages/config/src/index.ts (no ./generate re-export); generate.ts line 1 imports @valibot/to-json-schema

facts.title: Metro watches .env, but Fast Refresh won't apply it
facts.fact: Expo puts .env in Metro's watcher.additionalExts, so edits need no restart and no --clear. But EXPO_PUBLIC_ inlining is production-only; in dev a changed key lands only on a full app reload, not a Fast Refresh.
facts.source: @expo/metro-config 57.0.3 build/ExpoMetroConfig.js (watcher.additionalExts); babel-preset-expo 57.0.1 build/plugins/inline-env-vars.js (inlines only when api.caller(getIsProd); otherwise references expo/virtual/env); https://docs.expo.dev/guides/environment-variables/

facts.title: v5 needs no Babel preset and no jsxImportSource
facts.fact: Migrating from v4: drop `nativewind/babel` from your presets and drop `jsxImportSource: "nativewind"`. babel.config.js is plain `babel-preset-expo` — className is wired by a Metro import rewrite, not a JSX transform.

facts.title: Skip @import nativewind/theme and the RN variants vanish
facts.fact: Tailwind v4 is CSS-first: config lives in global.css. Without `@import "nativewind/theme"`, the RN-only variants and utilities silently do not exist.

facts.title: Metro rewrites react-native imports to add className
facts.fact: withNativewind sets `globalClassNamePolyfill: true`, so Metro resolves your `react-native` imports to `react-native-css/components`. That is the entire mechanism — any component Metro doesn't rewrite has no className.

facts.title: Only RN core components get className for free
facts.fact: Covered: View, Text, Image, ScrollView, TextInput, Pressable, TouchableOpacity, FlatList, Switch, Button, ActivityIndicator. Not covered: SectionList, Modal, SafeAreaView, expo-image, FlashList, Gluestack — wrap with styled().

facts.title: styled() replaces cssInterop() and remapProps()
facts.fact: Both v4 APIs are gone. Wrap third-party components with `styled`; the mapping defaults to `{ className: "style" }`, so a bare call is usually enough.

facts.title: ios:, android:, native: and web: are real variants
facts.fact: `native:` matches iOS and Android, never web. It is declared after `ios:`, so when both apply on iOS `native:` wins — order, not specificity.
facts.code:
<View className="ios:pt-12 android:pt-6" />

// Renders black on iOS: native: is declared
// after ios:, so it wins.
<Text
  className="ios:text-white native:text-black"
/>

facts.title: className styles the root; inner slots get sibling props
facts.fact: className hits the root style only. Inner slots take sibling props: contentContainerClassName, indicatorClassName, placeholderClassName.
facts.code:
<ScrollView
  className="flex-1"
  contentContainerClassName="gap-4 p-4"
/>

facts.title: Only five pseudo-classes compile; the rest are dropped
facts.fact: The selector compiler handles :hover, :active, :focus, :disabled and :empty. Any other pseudo-class (:first-child, :nth-child, :checked, :not) makes it discard the whole rule — silently, at build time.

facts.title: dark: is driven by Appearance, not a NativeWind API
facts.fact: v5 dropped the `colorScheme` export and deprecated nativewind's useColorScheme — use React Native's Appearance/useColorScheme instead.

facts.title: 1rem is 14px on native, not 16
facts.fact: rem is inlined to dp at build time with a 14 default, so `text-base` is fontSize 14. Don't port a 16px-baseline design straight across.
facts.code:
<Text className="text-base" />
// -> { fontSize: 14, lineHeight: 21 }

facts.title: NativeWind ships RN-only utilities Tailwind never had
facts.fact: nativewind/theme adds elevation-* (Android), ripple-* (android_ripple prop) and corner-squircle (iOS borderCurve). tint-* is a no-op in preview.4.
facts.code:
<Pressable
  className="elevation-md ripple-blue-500"
/>

facts.title: line-clamp-N compiles to the numberOfLines prop
facts.fact: The compiler maps -webkit-line-clamp onto Text's numberOfLines prop, so it truncates natively. It also emits overflow: hidden, nothing else.
facts.code:
<Text className="line-clamp-2 text-sm">
  {movie.overview}
</Text>
// -> numberOfLines={2}
//    style={{ overflow: "hidden" }}
facts.source: react-native-css 3.0.7 src/compiler/atRules.ts (default StyleRuleMapping); verified by compiling Tailwind v4 output through compile()

facts.title: pt-safe / pb-safe come free with nativewind/theme
facts.fact: nativewind/theme imports tailwindcss-safe-area. Insets resolve from SafeAreaProvider, which Expo Router already mounts — no useSafeAreaInsets needed.
facts.code:
<View className="pb-safe px-4">
  <Text>Watchlist</Text>
</View>

// inset plus spacing, one utility
<View className="pb-safe-offset-2" />
facts.source: nativewind/theme.css line 2; tailwindcss-safe-area 1.3.0; react-native-css 3.0.7 parseEnv + src/metro/resolver.ts (rewrites react-native-safe-area-context); expo-router ExpoRoot.js

facts.title: @map-[prop]: sends a utility's value to any prop
facts.fact: NativeWind-only variant: moves the value off `style` onto the named prop — for props react-native-css doesn't map already, like selectionColor.
facts.code:
<TextInput
  placeholder="Search movies"
  className="@map-[selectionColor]:color-red-500"
/>
// -> selectionColor="#fb2c36"
facts.source: nativewind 5.0.0-preview.4 src/plugin.tsx (matchVariant "@map"); react-native-css 3.0.7 src/compiler/atRules.ts ships built-in maps (caret-* -> cursorColor, line-clamp-* -> numberOfLines); verified by compiling

facts.title: Toggling an animate/group class remounts the subtree
facts.fact: Gaining a variable, container, animation or active state after the first render resets state and remounts children. Reserve with will-change-*.

facts.title: group-* compiles to a container query, not a selector
facts.fact: The parent registers as a named container and gains press handlers — a plain View is swapped to a Pressable, so you don't need to wrap it.
facts.code:
<View className="group">
  <Text className="group-active:text-red-500">
    {movie.title}
  </Text>
</View>
// View -> Pressable; group -> container query
facts.source: Verified compile: `group` -> {c:["g:group"]}; `group-active:` -> {cq:[{n:"g:group",p:{a:1}}]}. react-native-css 3.0.7 src/native/styles/index.ts (injects onPress) + src/native/react/useNativeCss.ts:141-143 (View -> Pressable)

facts.title: Only trust perf numbers from a release build
facts.fact: Hermes compiles the bundle to bytecode ahead of time only for release builds; in dev, Metro serves plain JS and the JS thread carries extra dev-only work. Dev-build timings tell you nothing about real TTI or frame drops.

facts.title: The Perf Monitor's two FPS rows tell you who is to blame
facts.fact: Press m in the Expo CLI terminal, then "Toggle performance monitor". It reports UI-thread and JS-thread FPS separately: a UI drop means native render/layout cost, a JS drop means your JavaScript is blocking.

facts.title: Press j for React Native DevTools, not Chrome DevTools
facts.fact: j in the Expo CLI terminal opens RN DevTools (Console, Sources, Network, Memory, Components, Profiler). The Profiler records JS only, attaches to debug builds only, and its frames are not yet symbolicated with sourcemaps.

facts.title: Animate with worklets so a busy JS thread can't stutter
facts.fact: useAnimatedStyle's body is a worklet: it runs on the UI runtime, not the JS thread, so heavy JS work can't drop your animation's frames.
facts.source: node_modules/react-native-reanimated (4.5.0 — useAnimatedStyle/useSharedValue; mutables.ts warns on writing .value during render)

facts.title: InteractionManager is a gutted no-op in RN 0.86
facts.fact: runAfterInteractions now just wraps setImmediate, createInteractionHandle returns -1, and setDeadline does nothing — the module is a deprecated stub. Old advice to defer work past animations with it no longer applies.

facts.title: measure() is synchronous, but only inside a worklet
facts.fact: measure() reads layout synchronously on the New Arch, but returns null on the JS runtime — schedule it onto the UI thread, after layout.

facts.title: Expo Atlas shows what each library costs in the bundle
facts.fact: EXPO_ATLAS=true records the module graph during export and the viewer breaks the bundle down by module. In dev, run expo start with it, then Shift+M.
facts.code:
EXPO_ATLAS=true pnpm expo export --platform ios
pnpm dlx expo-atlas .expo/atlas.jsonl

facts.title: Importing Reanimated alone costs 25-30% more memory
facts.fact: A Hermes change that shipped in RN 0.85 means merely importing react-native-reanimated raises app memory 25-30%, even if you never use it. Enabling worklets bundle mode is the documented workaround.

facts.title: performance.rnStartupTiming gives real startup numbers
facts.fact: RN 0.86's performance has an RN-only rnStartupTiming getter — startup ms from a release build. TS doesn't declare it, so cast; fields are nullable.

facts.title: Reanimated ships an FPS overlay that works in release
facts.fact: The dev-menu FPS monitor can't run in a release build. This is a plain component, so it ships in one — JS and UI FPS where the numbers are real.

facts.title: Function components take ref as a prop — drop forwardRef
facts.fact: React 19 passes ref to function components as a plain prop — forwardRef is no longer needed (not deprecated yet). Class components don't get it.

facts.title: Ref callbacks return cleanup; implicit returns now error
facts.fact: A ref callback may now return a cleanup function, so TypeScript rejects the concise arrow that implicitly returns an assignment. Use a braced body.
facts.code:
<View ref={(n) => (ref.current = n)} />  // TS error

<View ref={(n) => { ref.current = n; }} />

<View ref={(n) => {
  track(n);
  return () => untrack(n);
}} />

facts.title: Context object is the provider — render <Ctx value={…}>
facts.fact: In React 19 the context object is itself the provider — `Context<T> extends Provider<T>` in the types. `<Ctx.Provider>` still works and is not deprecated yet, but React says it will be, so use `<Ctx value={…}>` in new code.

facts.title: use() can be called conditionally, unlike other hooks
facts.fact: use(ctx) can sit inside an `if` or after an early return. But an uncached promise created during render throws — cache it (TanStack Query).

facts.title: Async startTransition: isPending spans the await
facts.fact: isPending goes true immediately, stays true across the await. React loses the async scope — set* calls after an await need a second startTransition.

facts.title: useOptimistic setters must run inside a transition
facts.fact: Call the setter inside startTransition or React dev-warns; the optimistic value auto-reverts to the real state once the action settles.
facts.source: react-native 0.86.0 ReactFabric-dev.js:7010 ("An optimistic state update occurred outside a transition or action.") + https://react.dev/blog/2024/12/05/react-19

facts.title: useEffectEvent (19.2) stops effects from re-running
facts.fact: New in 19.2, live in RN 0.86: reads the latest props/state without becoming a dep. Never list it in deps or call it in render — only from an Effect.
facts.source: https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent + react-native 0.86.0 ReactFabric-dev.js:6591 ("can't be called during rendering")

facts.title: <Activity mode="hidden"> keeps state, unmounts effects
facts.fact: Works in RN 0.86: hidden children keep state, have effects unmounted, and render at low priority — unlike unmounting a screen, which loses state.
facts.code:
import { Activity } from "react";

<Activity mode={open ? "visible" : "hidden"}>
  <SearchScreen />
</Activity>

facts.title: React Compiler is already on — skip manual memoization
facts.fact: app.json turns on experiments.reactCompiler, so components memoize automatically — hand-written useMemo/React.memo is now mostly redundant.

facts.title: useDeferredValue takes an initialValue second arg
facts.fact: useDeferredValue(value, initialValue) — the 19.0 second argument is what it returns on the very first render, so a search screen can paint an empty result list immediately instead of blocking on the first query.

facts.title: RN batches everything; there is no flushSync on native
facts.fact: Updates from native events, promises and timeouts all batch into one render. flushSync is react-dom-only and doesn't exist on native — react-native re-exports only unstable_batchedUpdates. Measure in useLayoutEffect.
facts.source: react-native 0.86.0 index.js:365 (unstable_batchedUpdates; no flushSync export) + ReactFabric-dev.js reconcilerVersion 19.2.3

facts.title: Nothing turns on StrictMode in an Expo Router app
facts.fact: Add <StrictMode> to the root layout yourself — the template does not. In React 19 it also double-invokes ref callbacks on mount, while useMemo/useCallback reuse the first render's memoized result.

facts.title: Flexbox defaults: column, flexShrink 0, not web's row
facts.fact: Yoga's defaults differ from CSS: flexDirection is column, alignContent is flex-start, flexShrink is 0. A Text beside an icon in a row overflows its container instead of truncating until you give it flex:1 or flexShrink:1.

facts.title: Style inheritance exists only inside Text subtrees
facts.fact: Every string must sit inside <Text>; a bare text node under <View> throws. Styles never cascade from View to Text — only nested Text inherits.

facts.title: Text is not selectable by default
facts.fact: RN text can't be highlighted or copied: Text's `selectable` prop defaults to false. Pass `selectable` on anything worth copying — an error code, an ID, an address.

facts.title: shadowOffset/shadowRadius are iOS-only; use boxShadow
facts.fact: The shadow* props are typed @platform ios; Android needs `elevation`. boxShadow is cross-platform under the New Arch — outset needs Android 9+, inset Android 10+.

facts.title: useWindowDimensions re-renders; Dimensions.get does not
facts.fact: Dimensions.get('window') is a one-shot snapshot. useWindowDimensions subscribes to the change event, so rotation and font-scale changes actually re-render your component.

facts.title: hitSlop never extends past the parent view's bounds
facts.fact: hitSlop grows a touch target, but RN clips it to the parent's frame — slop on a small icon inside a tightly-sized wrapper is silently discarded. Size the wrapper too.

facts.title: onLayout coords are parent-relative, and arrive late
facts.fact: e.nativeEvent.layout is {x, y, width, height} measured against the parent — not the screen — and only arrives after the native layout pass, so it is absent on first render.

facts.title: StyleSheet.hairlineWidth is the thinnest visible line
facts.fact: It resolves to PixelRatio.roundToNearestPixel(0.4), floored at one physical pixel. Use it for dividers — a literal borderWidth: 1 renders chunky on a 3x screen.

facts.title: keyboardWillShow never fires on Android — use DidShow
facts.fact: Only keyboardDidShow/DidHide fire on Android — the Will* events are iOS-only, so Will-based layout code silently never runs there. For a one-off read, Keyboard.isVisible() and Keyboard.metrics() are synchronous.

facts.title: Appearance.setColorScheme overrides the app's theme
facts.fact: Forces light/dark inside the app without changing system settings; useColorScheme() picks it up. Pass 'unspecified' to hand control back to the OS.

facts.title: Text scales with the OS font setting unless you cap it
facts.fact: allowFontScaling defaults to true, so the OS accessibility text size multiplies every fontSize. Cap it with maxFontSizeMultiplier (e.g. 1.3) on a Text rather than switching scaling off entirely.

facts.title: There is no legacy bridge left to opt into
facts.fact: Since 0.82 the New Architecture is the only runtime: Fabric renders, TurboModules load lazily over JSI, Codegen emits native glue from your TS specs. Old NativeModules libraries run through an interop layer, not a bridge.

facts.title: Host refs expose a synchronous getBoundingClientRect()
facts.fact: Refs to View/Text are ReactNativeElement nodes: getBoundingClientRect() returns a DOMRect synchronously, unlike callback-based measure().

facts.title: First tap on a search result is eaten by the keyboard
facts.fact: ScrollView and FlashList default keyboardShouldPersistTaps to 'never': with the keyboard up, a tap only dismisses it — the child gets no onPress.

facts.title: blurOnSubmit is deprecated: use submitBehavior
facts.fact: Single-line TextInput defaults to 'blurAndSubmit', so Enter closes the keyboard. 'submit' fires onSubmitEditing but keeps it open for the next query.

facts.title: Pick the TMDB image width from PixelRatio, not guesswork
facts.fact: getPixelSizeForLayoutSize(dp) returns physical px: a 115dp poster on a 3x phone is 345px. Snap to the nearest TMDB bucket (w342), not w185 (blurry).

facts.title: Pressable flashes 'pressed' when a scroll begins
facts.fact: onPressIn fires on touch-down and Pressability holds the pressed style for 130ms, so cards flash when a scroll starts. unstable_pressDelay defers it.

facts.title: Layout-only Views are deleted from the native tree
facts.fact: A View that only lays out children is dropped from the native hierarchy as an optimization, so nativeID lookups and native effects find nothing. collapsable={false} keeps it; collapsableChildren={false} keeps its direct children.

facts.title: Absolutely-positioned children ignore parent padding
facts.fact: Yoga keeps W3C-deviating errata on: an absolute child with no insets ignores parent padding, and its % lengths resolve against the content box.

facts.title: An <Svg> with no width/height fills its whole parent
facts.fact: Svg with neither width nor height silently defaults to 100%x100% of its parent (unless position is 'absolute'). Set both, or your 24px icon eats the screen.
facts.source: apps/mobile/node_modules/react-native-svg/src/elements/Svg.tsx (render(): width = height = '100%' when both undefined && position !== 'absolute')

facts.title: fill="currentColor" resolves to the Svg's color prop
facts.fact: fill/stroke accept 'currentColor', 'none' or 'url(#id)'. currentColor resolves to the nearest color prop — the cheap way to theme one icon.
facts.code:
<Svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width={20} height={20}
     color={active ? '#000' : '#888'}>
  <Path d={iconPath} fill="currentColor" />
</Svg>

facts.title: LinearGradient defaults to horizontal, not top-to-bottom
facts.fact: Its defaults are x1/y1/x2/y2 = 0%,0%,100%,0% — left-to-right, unlike CSS linear-gradient. Define it inside <Defs> with an id, then fill="url(#id)".
facts.code:
<Defs>
  <LinearGradient id="g"
    x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
    <Stop offset="0" stopColor="#000" />
    <Stop offset="1" stopColor="#fff" />
  </LinearGradient>
</Defs>
<Rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="url(#g)" />

facts.title: Metro won't turn a .svg file into a component by itself
facts.fact: Metro treats .svg as an asset: `import Logo from './logo.svg'` yields an asset ref, not a component. Component imports need react-native-svg-transformer (not installed here); otherwise inline with SvgXml or pre-convert via SVGR.

facts.title: SvgXml/SvgUri props override the file's own <svg> attrs
facts.fact: Your props override the parsed root <svg>, so width/height/color beat whatever the file declares. Both also take fallback and onError.

facts.title: Animate SVG with animatedProps, not style
facts.fact: d, r, fill and friends are props, not styles. Wrap the element with Animated.createAnimatedComponent and feed it useAnimatedProps.

facts.title: Root SvgCss/LocalSvg are throwing stubs — use the subpath
facts.fact: SvgCss, SvgCssUri, LocalSvg and inlineStyles export from the root as no-arg stubs that throw at render. Import from react-native-svg/css.
facts.code:
import { SvgCssUri } from 'react-native-svg/css';

<SvgCssUri uri={logoUrl} width={64} height={64} />
facts.source: apps/mobile/node_modules/react-native-svg/src/deprecated.tsx (`(): never` throwers) + src/ReactNativeSVG.ts (root re-export) + css/package.json subpath entry

facts.title: SVG <Image> defaults to width 0 and contain, not cover
facts.fact: width/height default to 0 — href alone renders nothing. preserveAspectRatio defaults to 'xMidYMid meet' (contain); use 'slice' for cover.
facts.code:
import { Image as SvgImage } from
  'react-native-svg';

<SvgImage
  href={{ uri: posterUrl }}
  width="100%" height="100%"
  preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice"
/>

facts.title: Mask is luminance-based: white keeps, black hides
facts.fact: mask="url(#id)" is a prop on any renderable element. Content is read as luminance, not alpha: white keeps, black hides. maskType="alpha" switches.
facts.code:
<Mask id="hole">
  <Rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#fff" />
  <Circle cx={24} cy={24} r={20} fill="#000" />
</Mask>
<Image href={{ uri: backdrop }} width="100%"
  height="100%" mask="url(#hole)" />
facts.source: apps/mobile/node_modules/react-native-svg/src/elements/Mask.tsx (maskType defaults to 'luminance') + src/lib/maskType.ts + src/lib/extract/types.ts (mask on CommonPathProps)

facts.title: Use one transform prop — the shorthands are deprecated
facts.fact: rotation, origin, scale, translate and skew are all @deprecated. Use transform — it takes an SVG string, an RN transform array, or a matrix.
facts.code:
<G transform="rotate(45 12 12)" />

<G transform={[{ translateX: 8 },
               { rotate: '45deg' }]} />

<G transform={[1, 0, 0, 1, 8, 8]} />

facts.title: getTotalLength is experimental — do the math yourself
facts.fact: getBBox, getCTM, getTotalLength and getPointAtLength are marked in-source as experimental and likely broken. Compute the circumference yourself.
facts.source: apps/mobile/node_modules/react-native-svg/src/elements/Shape.tsx ("native methods are experimental and likely broken")

facts.title: Reanimated 4 needs two packages; Expo wires the plugin
facts.fact: v4 is New-Architecture-only and splits worklets into react-native-worklets. The Babel plugin moved to react-native-worklets/plugin, but babel-preset-expo auto-adds it when the package resolves, so babel.config.js stays plain.

facts.title: Animated.View comes from Reanimated, not react-native
facts.fact: Reanimated's Animated exports only View, Text, Image, ScrollView, FlatList. Anything else (Pressable, expo-image) needs createAnimatedComponent.

facts.title: Shared values: use .get()/.set(), never .value in render
facts.fact: A shared value lives on the UI thread. Reading it in render gives a stale snapshot and never re-renders — read it inside useAnimatedStyle.

facts.title: useAnimatedStyle needs no dependency array on native
facts.fact: The Worklets Babel plugin serializes each worklet's captured variables onto __closure, and Reanimated derives the hook's inputs from that. The optional deps array is a web / no-plugin fallback, not something you maintain.

facts.title: withTiming defaults to 300ms and inOut(quad), not linear
facts.fact: withTiming's implicit config is { duration: 300, easing: Easing.inOut(Easing.quad) }. If you expected a constant rate you must pass Easing.linear yourself.

facts.title: withRepeat repeats twice by default — pass -1 for forever
facts.fact: withRepeat(anim) defaults to numberOfReps = 2 and reverse = false. A non-positive count means infinite, and reverse = true yoyos every other run.

facts.title: runOnJS is deprecated: scheduleOnRN takes args directly
facts.fact: In worklets 0.10 runOnJS/runOnUI are deprecated aliases. The replacements take arguments inline rather than returning a curried function.

facts.title: Reanimated 4 ships named spring presets
facts.fact: Gentle, Snappy and Wiggly spring configs are exported (plus *WithDuration variants), so you can stop hand-guessing stiffness and damping.

facts.title: interpolate extrapolates past the range unless you CLAMP
facts.fact: interpolate() defaults to Extrapolation.EXTEND: input past the range keeps extending the line (opacity goes negative). Pass CLAMP to pin the edges.

facts.title: entering/exiting/layout animate mount, unmount and moves
facts.fact: Layout animations need no shared values or worklets — they are props on an Animated component, with builders you chain config onto.

facts.title: Reanimated 4 has CSS animations: animationName in style
facts.fact: A new declarative engine: put animationName keyframes plus animationDuration in an Animated component's style. No shared value, no worklet, no hook.
facts.code:
<Animated.View
  style={{
    animationName: {
      from: { opacity: 0 },
      to: { opacity: 1 },
    },
    animationDuration: '300ms',
  }}
/>;

facts.title: useAnimatedReaction is the shared-value side-effect hook
facts.fact: useAnimatedReaction(prepare, react) re-runs prepare when a shared value in its closure changes, then calls react(current, previous) — how you fire haptics or a scheduleOnRN callback off a shared value without touching render.

facts.title: sharedTransitionTag is a silent no-op by default
facts.fact: Gated by a flag compiled into the native binary, default false. Set it in apps/mobile/package.json — not the workspace root — then rebuild natively.

facts.title: System Reduce Motion turns animations into instant jumps
facts.fact: With the OS setting on, withTiming/withSpring jump straight to toValue on frame one. Opt out per animation, or app-wide with <ReducedMotionConfig>.

facts.title: skipEntering stops the whole list animating in at mount
facts.fact: entering fires for every item on first mount, so the whole grid pops in at once. Wrap the list and only later insertions animate.

facts.title: useScrollOffset returns one axis: x wins unless x is 0
facts.fact: Renamed from useScrollViewOffset. Taps the animated ref directly — no onScroll prop — but yields one number: contentOffset.x, and y only when x is 0.

facts.title: transitionProperty tweens plain state-driven style changes
facts.fact: Unlike animationName keyframes: put transitionProperty + a duration in an Animated component's style, and any re-render that changes it tweens.
facts.code:
<Animated.View
  style={{
    opacity: liked ? 1 : 0.4,
    transitionProperty: 'opacity',
    transitionDuration: 300,
  }}
/>;

facts.title: RN 0.86 deprecates the built-in SafeAreaView
facts.fact: Importing SafeAreaView from 'react-native' now warns "deprecated and will be removed", and it never applied insets on Android anyway. Import SafeAreaView from react-native-safe-area-context instead.

facts.title: Expo Router already mounts SafeAreaProvider for you
facts.fact: ExpoRoot wraps the whole app in <SafeAreaProvider>, so useSafeAreaInsets() works in any route with zero setup. A second provider in your root _layout.tsx is redundant — it just re-measures and re-provides the same insets.

facts.title: SafeAreaView pads all four edges unless you say otherwise
facts.fact: With no `edges` prop every edge defaults to 'additive'. A Stack's native header already consumes the top inset, so opt out or you double-pad the top.
facts.code:
<SafeAreaView edges={["left", "right", "bottom"]}>
  <MovieDetail />
</SafeAreaView>

facts.title: Never wrap a scrolling list in SafeAreaView
facts.fact: SafeAreaView pads the list's outer box, so rows clip above the home indicator instead of scrolling under it. Put the inset in contentContainerStyle.
facts.source: react-native-safe-area-context 5.7.0 src/SafeArea.types.ts (mode defaults to 'padding'); FlashList 2.0.2 FlashListProps extends ScrollViewProps

facts.title: enableScreens is already on; enableFreeze is not
facts.fact: react-native-screens turns itself on at import, so the enableScreens() call old tutorials show is a no-op. Freezing is separate: enableFreeze() still defaults to false — call it to stop blurred screens re-rendering.
facts.source: react-native-screens 4.25.2 src/core.ts (ENABLE_SCREENS = isNativePlatformSupported; ENABLE_FREEZE = false); src/components/Screen.tsx (freezeOnBlur = freezeEnabled())

facts.title: Use edges mode 'maximum' for a minimum bottom gap
facts.fact: 'maximum' means max(safeArea, padding) instead of the sum — the right mode for a floating button that needs a minimum gap on every device.
facts.code:
<SafeAreaView
  edges={{ bottom: "maximum", top: "off" }}
  style={{ paddingBottom: 16 }}
>
  <FavoriteButton />
</SafeAreaView>

facts.title: useSafeAreaInsets throws in Jest — use the shipped mock
facts.fact: useSafeAreaInsets() throws with no provider, and SafeAreaProvider renders null children until a native event Jest never fires. Mock it in setup.
facts.source: react-native-safe-area-context 5.7.0 jest/mock.tsx; src/SafeAreaContext.tsx (useSafeAreaInsets throws NO_INSETS_ERROR; provider renders children only when insets != null)

facts.title: mode="margin" keeps the background out of the inset
facts.fact: Default mode='padding' paints the view's background behind the home indicator. mode='margin' offsets the box instead — what a floating card wants.
facts.code:
<SafeAreaView
  mode="margin"
  edges={["bottom"]}
  className="mx-4 rounded-2xl bg-card"
>
  <AddToWatchlistBar />
</SafeAreaView>
facts.source: react-native-safe-area-context 5.7.0 src/specs/NativeSafeAreaView.ts (mode: WithDefault<'padding'|'margin','padding'>); common/cpp RNCSafeAreaViewShadowNode.cpp (margin mode reads yogaStyle.margin)

facts.title: A toast needs FullWindowOverlay to sit above a sheet
facts.fact: A toast in your screen tree renders beneath a formSheet. FullWindowOverlay escapes above it — iOS only; on Android it warns and renders a plain View.

facts.title: v4 has no tailwind.config.js — the theme lives in CSS
facts.fact: Config is CSS-first: @import "tailwindcss", then declare tokens in an @theme block. A legacy JS config loads only if you opt in with @config.

facts.title: Only @theme vars mint utilities — :root vars do not
facts.fact: A --color-mint-500 inside @theme auto-generates bg-mint-500, text-mint-500, border-mint-500. The same var in :root generates nothing — it stays a plain CSS variable Tailwind never turns into classes.

facts.title: One --spacing variable drives every size utility
facts.fact: p-4 compiles to calc(var(--spacing) * 4), so any integer works — p-13 is valid with zero config. Default --spacing is 0.25rem.

facts.title: Class names must appear as complete strings in source
facts.fact: v4 scans your source as plain text, so `text-${color}-600` never generates a class. Write class names in full, or safelist with @source inline().
facts.code: @source inline("bg-red-{100..900..100}");

facts.title: @import "tailwindcss" = theme + preflight + utilities
facts.fact: Import the three parts individually to drop preflight — but keep layer(utilities), or the utilities emit unlayered and outrank every layered rule.

facts.title: Use @theme inline when a token points at another var
facts.fact: Plain @theme freezes the token in :root, so a scoped .dark override never reaches it. @theme inline substitutes the value into the utility instead.
facts.source: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/theme; confirmed by compiling both forms with tailwindcss@4.3.2

facts.title: Custom utilities go in @utility, not @layer utilities
facts.fact: A class inside @layer utilities is plain CSS — it gets no variants (dark:, lg:) and sorts wrong. @utility registers a real utility.

facts.title: bg-(--card) is the v4 shorthand for bg-[var(--card)]
facts.fact: Parentheses around a custom property drop the var() wrapper in arbitrary values. Square brackets still take raw values.
facts.source: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/adding-custom-styles; verified bg-(--card) compiles to background-color: var(--card)

facts.title: --color-*: initial deletes the whole default palette
facts.fact: Set a namespace to initial and every default utility in it stops compiling; only your tokens survive. --*: initial wipes every namespace at once.

facts.title: Unused theme tokens are stripped; @theme static keeps them
facts.fact: Tailwind emits only the theme vars its utilities reference, so a token you read via var() at runtime gets dropped. @theme static emits every one.
facts.source: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/theme; verified by compiling both @theme and @theme static with tailwindcss@4.3.2

facts.title: One --text-* token can carry line-height, weight, tracking
facts.fact: A --text-<name> token accepts --line-height, --font-weight and --letter-spacing suffixes; the single class text-<name> then emits all four properties.
facts.source: tailwindcss@4.3.2/theme.css (--text-xs--line-height etc.); verified text-title emits all 4 props

facts.title: theme() is deprecated: --spacing(), --alpha(), --theme()
facts.fact: These v4 CSS functions work inside your own rules and @utility blocks. v3's theme() is deprecated in favor of them but still compiles at 4.3.2.
facts.source: https://tailwindcss.com/docs/functions-and-directives; all three verified compiling at 4.3.2, where theme() is deprecated but not removed

facts.title: bg-card/80 compiles to color-mix, and it works on native
facts.fact: The /opacity modifier emits color-mix() behind an @supports probe. react-native-css whitelists that exact probe string, so it resolves on device.
facts.code:
<View className="bg-card/80" />

// background-color: rgb(var(--card));
// @supports (color:
//   color-mix(in lab, red, red)) {
//   background-color: color-mix(in oklab,
//     rgb(var(--card)) 80%, transparent);
// }
facts.source: Compiled bg-card/80 against the repo's @theme inline tokens; react-native-css@3.0.7 src/compiler/supports.ts

facts.title: isLoading is literally isFetching && isPending
facts.fact: isFetching = queryFn in flight (including background refetch). isPending = no data in cache. isLoading is exactly isFetching && isPending: a query with enabled:false and no data is isPending but never isLoading — don't spin on it.

facts.title: Use skipToken, not enabled, when an arg may be undefined
facts.fact: skipToken disables a query like enabled:false, but keeps queryFn's types honest — no non-null assertion when the id may be undefined.

facts.title: keepPreviousData is now a function, not an option
facts.fact: v5 deleted the keepPreviousData option. Pass the keepPreviousData FUNCTION as placeholderData; result.isPlaceholderData flags the stale page.
facts.code:
import { keepPreviousData } from
  "@tanstack/react-query"

useQuery({
  queryKey: ["search", term],
  queryFn: () => search(term),
  placeholderData: keepPreviousData,
})

facts.title: mutate never rejects; mutateAsync does
facts.fact: mutate() returns void and never rejects — it catches internally, so errors surface only via result.error / onError. mutateAsync() returns the real promise and WILL reject, so it needs a try/catch. Prefer mutate.

facts.title: invalidateQueries is prefix-matched and active-only
facts.fact: invalidateQueries matches by key PREFIX: { queryKey: ['tmdb'] } hits ['tmdb','movie',42]; add exact:true to target one key. Only ACTIVE (mounted) queries refetch — inactive ones are just marked stale, unless refetchType: 'all'.

facts.title: refetchOnWindowFocus does nothing until you wire AppState
facts.fact: Defaults to true, but focusManager's listener needs window.addEventListener, which RN lacks — no focus event ever fires until you bridge AppState.

facts.title: On RN, onlineManager thinks you are always online
facts.fact: Its default listener never attaches in RN and it starts online:true, so offline pausing and paused mutations never happen — bridge expo-network.

facts.title: The official devtools package does not run on React Native
facts.fact: @tanstack/react-query-devtools renders DOM, so it will not run in an RN app. Use rn-better-dev-tools (a macOS app) or reactotron-react-query instead.

facts.title: queryOptions() makes getQueryData type itself
facts.fact: queryOptions() brands the queryKey with DataTag, so getQueryData / setQueryData infer the data type — no generics, no duplicated key literals.
facts.code:
export const movieQuery = (id: number) =>
  queryOptions({
    queryKey: ["tmdb", "movie", id],
    queryFn: () => getMovie(id),
  })

useQuery(movieQuery(id))
qc.getQueryData(movieQuery(id).queryKey) // typed

facts.title: useInfiniteQuery needs initialPageParam + getNextPageParam
facts.fact: v5 requires both. Return undefined from getNextPageParam for 'no more pages' (hasNextPage: false); data comes back as { pages, pageParams }.
facts.code:
useInfiniteQuery({
  queryKey: ["tmdb", "popular"],
  queryFn: ({ pageParam }) => getPopular(pageParam),
  initialPageParam: 1,
  getNextPageParam: (last, _all, page) =>
    page < last.total_pages ? page + 1 : undefined,
})

facts.title: Guard fetchNextPage — onEndReached fires repeatedly
facts.fact: Flatten pages for FlashList, and gate fetchNextPage on hasNextPage && !isFetchingNextPage or you queue duplicate page fetches while scrolling.

facts.title: Optimistic updates: cancelQueries before you write
facts.fact: An in-flight refetch can land after your optimistic write and clobber it. Cancel first, snapshot, write; roll back in onError.

facts.title: select is only memoized if the function is stable
facts.fact: select narrows what a component re-renders on, but its result is cached only when the select fn is referentially identical between renders. An inline arrow re-runs every render — hoist it to module scope or wrap in useCallback.

facts.title: staleTime: 'static' opts a query out of invalidation too
facts.fact: 'static' data is never stale: no refetch on mount, focus, or reconnect — and invalidateQueries skips it entirely. Only an explicit refetch() or setQueryData updates it. A good fit for TMDB /configuration.

facts.title: subscribed: false parks a query for off-screen tabs
facts.fact: It detaches the observer — no refetches and no re-renders while a screen is off-stage, though cached data still renders. Drive it with useIsFocused, which Expo Router re-exports (no @react-navigation import needed).

facts.title: Refetching an infinite query refetches every loaded page
facts.fact: Invalidating a 10-page list re-runs all 10 requests, sequentially. maxPages caps the stored pages — and the storm; pair it with getPreviousPageParam.
facts.code:
useInfiniteQuery({
  ...popularInfinite(),
  initialPageParam: 1,
  getNextPageParam: (l, _a, p) =>
    p < l.total_pages ? p + 1 : undefined,
  getPreviousPageParam: (_f, _a, p) =>
    p > 1 ? p - 1 : undefined,
  maxPages: 5,
})
facts.source: @tanstack/query-core 5.101.2 src/infiniteQueryBehavior.ts:94-107 (do…while over oldPages.length) + :73-79 with utils.ts:418-426 (maxPages slice) + docs/framework/react/reference/useInfiniteQuery.md

facts.title: Honor TMDB's Retry-After via retryDelay(count, error)
facts.fact: Defaults: 3 retries, delay min(1000 * 2**n, 30_000). Both retry and retryDelay receive the error, so skip 404s and pace 429s off the header.
facts.code:
new QueryClient({ defaultOptions: { queries: {
  retry: (n, e: TmdbError) =>
    e.status !== 404 && n < 3,
  retryDelay: (n, e: TmdbError) =>
    e.retryAfter
      ? e.retryAfter * 1000
      : Math.min(1000 * 2 ** n, 30_000),
} } })
facts.source: @tanstack/query-core 5.101.2 src/retryer.ts:49-51 (defaultRetryDelay) + :170-179 (retry ?? 3; ShouldRetryFunction / RetryDelayFunction both called with (failureCount, error))

facts.title: v5 has no per-query onError — QueryCache has it
facts.fact: useQuery dropped onSuccess/onError in v5; mutations kept theirs. The global seam is QueryCache's onError(error, query) — report every failure there.
facts.source: @tanstack/query-core 5.101.2 src/queryCache.ts:19-30 (QueryCacheConfig) — types.ts declares onSuccess/onError only under MutationOptions (:1113-1135) and MutateOptions (:1160-1178)

facts.title: setQueryData(key, undefined) is a no-op, not a clear
facts.fact: Passing or returning undefined bails out, leaving the cache untouched. To actually drop data (logout), use removeQueries or resetQueries.
facts.code:
qc.setQueryData(key, undefined)        // no-op
qc.setQueryData(key, () => undefined)  // no-op

qc.removeQueries({ queryKey: key })    // gone
