facts.source: @tanstack/query-core 5.101.2 src/queryClient.ts:198-202 (const data = functionalUpdate(updater, prevData); if (data === undefined) return undefined — returns before queryCache.build().setData())

facts.title: Spreading a useQuery result re-renders on every field
facts.fact: The result is a Proxy: v5 re-renders only on the props you read. A rest-spread or {...q} touches every key, so you also re-render on fetchStatus.
facts.source: @tanstack/query-core 5.101.2 src/queryObserver.ts:263-291 (trackResult Proxy get → trackProp) + :666-698 (notify gates on #trackedProps)

facts.title: Seed a detail screen with placeholderData, not initialData
facts.fact: initialData lands in the cache as fresh data, so a nonzero staleTime skips the real fetch. placeholderData is never cached — the fetch still runs.
facts.source: @tanstack/query-core 5.101.2 src/query.ts:749-774 (initialData → state.data, dataUpdatedAt = Date.now(), status 'success') + queryObserver.ts:486-525 (placeholderData applied only while data === undefined && pending, never dispatched to state)

facts.title: RNTL v14: render, fireEvent and act all return Promises
facts.fact: React 19's async model: render, rerender, unmount, renderHook, fireEvent and act all return Promises. Miss an await and you assert on a stale tree.

facts.title: Prefer userEvent over fireEvent for real interactions
facts.fact: fireEvent just calls the onX prop, even on composites. userEvent replays RN's sequence (pressIn/pressOut, changeText, blur) on host elements only.

facts.title: getByRole with the name option is the first-choice query
facts.fact: Priority: *ByRole (+name) > label/placeholder/display-value > *ByText > *ByTestId last. RN has no implicit roles — set role="button" yourself.
facts.code:
<Pressable role="button" onPress={onSave}>
  <Text>Save</Text>
</Pressable>;

screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Save" });

facts.title: RNTL ships its Jest matchers — no jest-native import
facts.fact: Importing from @testing-library/react-native auto-extends expect — no jest-native install. There is no toBeInTheDocument; use toBeOnTheScreen(). Also built in: toBeVisible, toBeDisabled, toBeChecked, toHaveStyle, toHaveProp.

facts.title: A fresh QueryClient with retry: false in every test
facts.fact: TanStack Query retries 3x with backoff, so an error-path test times out instead of failing. Call the factory per test — a shared client leaks cache.

facts.title: Press handlers get a nativeEvent — use objectContaining
facts.fact: fireEvent.press and fireEvent.scroll always build a default nativeEvent and deep-merge whatever you pass, so your handler receives an event object. Assert with expect.objectContaining, not an exact object.

facts.title: jest-expo mocks Expo natives and already handles pnpm
facts.fact: jest-expo mocks the ExpoModulesCore native proxy (expo-* JS APIs become jest.fn stubs), transforms assets, and its transformIgnorePatterns already whitelists .pnpm — don't hand-write that regex.

facts.title: Fake timers: waitFor and userEvent advance them for you
facts.fact: userEvent mirrors RN press timing (press 130ms, longPress 500ms). Enable jest.useFakeTimers() — waitFor and userEvent advance them for you.

facts.title: Bare strings outside <Text> now throw in tests
facts.fact: v14 always validates that strings render inside <Text> — the unstable_validateStringsRenderedWithinText opt-out is gone. A bare string in a View now throws the same Invariant Violation you'd hit at runtime.

facts.title: Maestro flows are YAML: appId, ---, then commands
facts.fact: E2E flows are YAML in .maestro/, run against an installed dev build: maestro test .maestro/smoke.yaml. Install the CLI via brew, not npm.
facts.code:
appId: com.example.app
---
- launchApp
- tapOn: "Search"
- inputText: "Dune"
- assertVisible: "Dune: Part Two"

facts.title: Snapshot tests are the wrong tool for RN UI
facts.fact: Expo's own guidance: prefer end-to-end tests over snapshot tests for UI. RN tree snapshots churn on every style tweak and assert nothing a user cares about. Assert behavior with role/text queries; leave screens to Maestro.

facts.title: renderRouter: keep the handle, don't await it away
facts.fact: renderRouter returns a Promise that also carries the route getters. Keep the handle, await it, then read getPathname() off the handle itself.
facts.source: apps/mobile/node_modules/expo-router/build/testing-library/index.js + expect.js; verified by running a throwaway test in apps/mobile

facts.title: Don't add @shopify/flash-list/jestSetup — it breaks v2
facts.fact: Its jestSetup.js aliases FlashList to a RecyclerView export v2.0.2 lacks, so render throws 'Element type is invalid'. Skip it — v2 needs no setup.
facts.source: apps/mobile/node_modules/@shopify/flash-list/jestSetup.js + src/index.ts; verified by running both configs

facts.title: Queries skip elements hidden from accessibility
facts.fact: No query matches an element under display:'none', aria-hidden, or a sibling of an aria-modal view — an open modal hides the screen behind it.
facts.source: apps/mobile/node_modules/@testing-library/react-native/docs/api/accessibility.md + configuration.md; verified by running a test

facts.title: Every jest-expo test runs as iOS unless you say otherwise
facts.fact: Base preset pins platform ios: Platform.OS is 'ios' and .android.tsx never loads. A projects array runs both; jest-expo/universal adds web+node.
facts.source: apps/mobile/node_modules/jest-expo/src/resolveBabelOptions.js + config/getPlatformPreset.js; verified by running both presets

facts.title: Maestro text selectors are regexes, and waits are short
facts.fact: Every text selector is a regex — escape ( ) [ ] in titles. assertVisible gives up after ~7s, so wrap network-backed content in extendedWaitUntil.
facts.code:
- extendedWaitUntil:
    visible: "Dune: Part Two"
    timeout: 15000
- tapOn:
    text: "Spider-Man \\(2002\\)"

facts.title: v3 auth: api_key query param (Bearer works, unused here)
facts.fact: v3 accepts ?api_key=<32-char key> or an Authorization: Bearer token; this app uses api_key only. A wrong key returns HTTP 401, status_code 7.
facts.source: developer.themoviedb.org/docs/authentication-application (v3 takes api_key or a Bearer access token; same access); apps/mobile/src/lib/tmdb/client.ts + AGENTS.md (v3 api_key only); live GET /3/movie/550?api_key=deadbeef → 401 status_code 7

facts.title: append_to_response merges sub-resources as top-level keys
facts.fact: Up to 20 comma-separated values. Slashes stay in the key, so watch/providers appends as d["watch/providers"], still wrapped in { results }.

facts.title: Image file_path already carries its leading slash
facts.fact: Build {base}/t/p/{size}{file_path} — never insert a slash. A null file_path is common, so return null and let callers render a placeholder. Sizes come from /configuration.
facts.source: apps/mobile/src/lib/tmdb/images.ts; live GET /3/configuration → images.secure_base_url + poster_sizes w92…w780,original; live GET /3/movie/550 → poster_path "/jSziioSwPVrOy9Yow3XhWIBDjq1.jpg"

facts.title: List endpoints return genre_ids, not genre names
facts.fact: Lists (/movie/popular, /discover, /search) return genre_ids: number[]; names only on /movie/{id}. Cache /genre/movie/list and map ids yourself.
facts.source: live GET /3/movie/popular (results[0].genre_ids only, no genres) vs GET /3/movie/550 (genres[{id,name}], no genre_ids); apps/mobile/src/lib/tmdb/schemas.ts

facts.title: page maxes at 500 even when total_pages says 42,000
facts.fact: page=501 returns HTTP 400, status_code 22 ("Pages start at 1 and max at 500") even though total_pages is often far larger. Clamp getNextPageParam.
facts.code:
getNextPageParam: (last) =>
  last.page < Math.min(last.total_pages, 500)
    ? last.page + 1
    : undefined,

facts.title: search/multi results carry media_type — branch on it
facts.fact: Movies have title, TV has name, people have profile_path not poster_path. A shared card reading title/poster_path renders blank rows for people.
facts.source: .claude/skills/tmdb/SKILL.md §3; live GET /3/search/multi?query=nolan (person items have name + profile_path, no poster_path)

facts.title: with_watch_providers does nothing without watch_region
facts.fact: discover/movie?with_watch_providers=8 returns the whole catalog (~1.16M results) — the filter is silently dropped, not an error. Add watch_region=US and the same call returns ~4.7k. Always send both.
facts.source: live GET /3/discover/movie?with_watch_providers=8 (total_results 1,159,732 vs 1,159,656 unfiltered) vs …&watch_region=US (4,691); SKILL.md §4

facts.title: In discover params, comma means AND and pipe means OR
facts.fact: with_genres=28,878 means Action AND Fantasy; with_genres=28|878 means either. The same rule applies to with_watch_providers, with_companies and with_keywords — a stray comma silently narrows results toward zero.
facts.source: developer.themoviedb.org/reference/discover-movie ("Comma's are treated like an AND query while pipe's are treated like an OR"); live: 28,878 → 5,525 results vs 28|878 → 70,282; SKILL.md §4

facts.title: Rate limit is ~40 req/sec; the old 40-per-10s cap is gone
facts.fact: The 40-req/10s cap was dropped in 2019; the ceiling now sits around 40 req/sec and can move. Over it you get 429, and Retry-After isn't promised — back off exponentially and lean on TanStack Query's dedup/caching.
facts.source: developer.themoviedb.org/docs/rate-limiting (original 40/10s limit disabled Dec 16 2019; "somewhere in the 40 requests per second range"); SKILL.md §9

facts.title: Watch providers: no URLs, and JustWatch must be credited
facts.fact: Provider objects carry only provider_id/name/logo_path — no URL — so deep-link the country entry's TMDB `link`. And credit JustWatch wherever the data appears: TMDB revokes access if you don't.
facts.source: developer.themoviedb.org/reference/movie-watch-providers (attribution required; "not going to return full deep links"); live GET /3/movie/550/watch/providers → results.US.link + rent[] with no URL field

facts.title: Guest sessions can rate, but not watchlist or favorite
facts.fact: GET /authentication/guest_session/new returns guest_session_id + expires_at. Guests can rate only — watchlist/favorite writes need a v3 session_id.
facts.source: developer.themoviedb.org/docs/authentication-guest-sessions (guests "can only rate a movie, TV show and TV episode"); live POST /3/movie/550/rating?guest_session_id=… → status_code 1, POST /3/account/1/watchlist with guest_session_id → 401 status_code 3; SKILL.md §1/§10

facts.title: One page param pages every appended sub-resource at once
facts.fact: Appended resources honor the parent's query params: ?append_to_response=recommendations,similar&page=2 moves both to page 2 at once, with no per-resource form. For independent paging, call /movie/{id}/recommendations directly.
facts.source: developer.themoviedb.org/docs/append-to-response ("Each method will still respond to whatever query parameters are supported by each individual request"); live GET /3/movie/550?append_to_response=recommendations,similar&page=2 → both come back page 2; recommendations.page=2 is ignored

facts.title: v3 login: request token → browser approve → session_id
facts.fact: token/new → open https://www.themoviedb.org/authenticate/{token}?redirect_to={deepLink} with WebBrowser.openAuthSessionAsync → POST /authentication/session/new {request_token} → session_id. Tokens expire in 60 min if unused.
facts.source: developer.themoviedb.org/reference/authentication-how-do-i-generate-a-session-id (3 steps; redirect_to param; token auto-expires after 60 minutes if unused); live GET /3/authentication/token/new → expires_at +60 min; SKILL.md §1/§10

facts.title: language=en-US on /images deletes the textless backdrops
facts.fact: Backdrops are mostly iso_639_1: null (no burned-in title). language=en-US kept 11 of 102 for movie 550; include_image_language=en,null kept 86.
facts.source: live GET /3/movie/550/images → 102 backdrops, most with iso_639_1:null; ?language=en-US → 11 backdrops / 51 posters; ?include_image_language=en,null → 86 backdrops / 57 posters; developer.themoviedb.org/reference/movie-images

facts.title: TV: /credits is top-billing only; use aggregate_credits
facts.fact: /tv/{id}/credits is top-billing only — 8 rows for tv/1396. aggregate_credits gave 348, with roles[] {character, episode_count} and no character key.
facts.source: live GET /3/tv/1396/credits → cast.length 8, cast[0].character "Walter White", no roles; GET /3/tv/1396/aggregate_credits → cast.length 348, cast[0].roles[0] {character, episode_count 62}, total_episode_count 62, no character field; SKILL.md §2

facts.title: append_to_response takes sub-paths like season/1
facts.fact: Sub-paths work, not just names: append_to_response=season/1,season/2 inlines season objects with episodes[], replacing one call per season. 20 max.
facts.source: live GET /3/tv/1396?append_to_response=season/1,season/2,season/3 → response keys include "season/1","season/2","season/3", each a full season object (S1 has episodes[7], first "Pilot"); 21 append values → 400 status_code 27 "maximum number of remote calls is 20"; SKILL.md §2

facts.title: Ratings must be a multiple of 0.5, and 0 is rejected
facts.fact: 7.3 → HTTP 400, status_code 18 (validation failed). 0 and 10.5 are rejected too; valid is 0.5–10.0 in 0.5 steps. Success returns HTTP 201, not 200.
facts.source: live POST /3/movie/550/rating?guest_session_id=…: value 7.5 / 0.5 / 10 → HTTP 201; 7.3 → 400 status_code 18; 0 and -1 → 400 (must be greater than 0.0); 10.5 → 400 (must be ≤ 10.0); developer.themoviedb.org/docs/errors (18 = HTTP 400 validation failed) + reference/movie-add-rating (201)

facts.title: release_dates.results is an array, not country-keyed
facts.fact: watch/providers keys results by country; release_dates returns an array. Premiere rows (type 1) have certification "" — take the first non-empty one.
facts.source: live GET /3/movie/550/release_dates → results is an array of {iso_3166_1, release_dates[]}; the US entry starts with two type-1 premieres carrying certification "" before the first non-empty "R" row; contrast GET /3/movie/550/watch/providers → results is an object keyed by country code

facts.title: noUncheckedIndexedAccess: a length check does not narrow
facts.fact: With noUncheckedIndexedAccess on, every arr[i] and Record lookup is T | undefined — and a length check does not narrow it.
facts.source: Reproduced with tsc 6.0.3 (strict + noUncheckedIndexedAccess): TS2532 at arr[i]. Flag is on in tsconfig.base.json

facts.title: Iterate instead of indexing to dodge the | undefined
facts.fact: for..of and .map callbacks yield T, not T | undefined — the flag only affects index access. Prefer iterating, a destructuring default, or ??.
facts.code:
for (const m of movies) m.title; // Movie
movies.map((m) => m.title);      // Movie
const [top = EMPTY] = movies;    // Movie
const top2 = movies[0] ?? EMPTY; // Movie
facts.source: Verified with tsc 6.0.3 (noUncheckedIndexedAccess): all four bindings type-check as Movie, not Movie | undefined

facts.title: strict is ON by default in TypeScript 6
facts.fact: TS 6 flipped the default: an unspecified strict is treated as true (the compiler checks strict !== false). Opting out now needs an explicit "strict": false, and individual strict flags still override the group.
facts.source: getStrictOptionValue in typescript@6.0.3/lib/typescript.js:22255; reproduced — no strict key errors TS7006, "strict": false compiles clean

facts.title: types no longer auto-includes every @types package
facts.fact: TS 6 stops pulling in everything under node_modules/@types. If "types" is unset it resolves to [] — list what you need ("types": ["node"]) or restore the old behavior with the new wildcard "types": ["*"].
facts.source: getAutomaticTypeDirectiveNames + usesWildcardTypes in typescript@6.0.3/lib/typescript.js:44672, 21973; reproduced against a scratch @types package

facts.title: baseUrl is now an error — paths works without it
facts.fact: baseUrl is a deprecation error in TS 6 (TS5101) and is removed in TS 7. paths stands alone: its patterns resolve relative to the tsconfig that declares them.
facts.source: Reproduced with tsc 6.0.3: "Option 'baseUrl' is deprecated and will stop functioning in TypeScript 7.0" (TS5101); a paths-only config resolves @/* clean

facts.title: isolatedModules: re-exporting a type needs export type
facts.fact: isolatedModules is on and Metro/Babel transpile file-by-file, so a type can't ride a value re-export — that's TS1205. Use the inline type modifier.
facts.source: Reproduced with tsc 6.0.3 + isolatedModules: TS1205 on the plain re-export; both corrected forms compile clean

facts.title: satisfies checks a value without widening it
facts.fact: satisfies checks a value against a type but keeps its literal types — typo check and narrow union at once. A line break before it is a parse error.
facts.source: Verified with tsc 6.0.3: Poster is "w185" | "w500"; a newline before satisfies fails with TS1434, same restriction as `as`

facts.title: erasableSyntaxOnly bans enum, namespace, param properties
facts.fact: erasableSyntaxOnly (opt-in) rejects any construct that emits runtime code — enum, instantiated namespace, constructor parameter properties, import x = require() — all as TS1294. Model them as `as const` objects plus union types.
facts.source: Reproduced with tsc 6.0.3: TS1294 on enum, instantiated namespace, and a constructor parameter property (declare namespace is fine)

facts.title: NoInfer<T> pins a type param to the arg you mean
facts.fact: NoInfer<T> is a compiler intrinsic in lib.es5.d.ts: it blocks a position from contributing to inference, so T is fixed only by the arguments you want.
facts.source: type NoInfer<T> = intrinsic — typescript@6.0.3/lib/lib.es5.d.ts:1678; TS2345 on the number arg reproduced with tsc 6.0.3

facts.title: filter(m => m !== null) narrows; filter(Boolean) does not
facts.fact: TS infers a type predicate from the callback, so the drop-bad-rows pattern narrows itself. filter(Boolean) does NOT — it yields (Movie | null)[].
facts.source: Reproduced with tsc 6.0.3 under the repo's tsconfig.base.json settings (strict + noUncheckedIndexedAccess): assigning the filter(Boolean) result to Movie[] fails TS2322; the !== null form type-checks. Reassigning the callback param inside the body also disables the inference.

facts.title: Index-access narrowing needs a const key, not a let
facts.fact: noUncheckedIndexedAccess narrowing survives a guard only if the index is a const reference. A let — even one never reassigned — blocks it.
facts.source: Reproduced with tsc 6.0.3 + noUncheckedIndexedAccess (as in tsconfig.base.json): the const-key form compiles; the let-key form errors TS2532 'Object is possibly undefined'. Same holds for arr[i].

facts.title: const type params keep call-site literal types
facts.fact: A const type param keeps call-site literals — no as const per call. Only earns its keep on a wide constraint; a literal union infers them anyway.
facts.source: Verified with tsc 6.0.3: keyof typeof d is "credits" | "videos" (assigning "images" to it errors TS2322), and the same signature without the const modifier widens the key type to string. The widening only happens because the constraint is wide: with a literal-union constraint (readonly Extra[]) TS already infers the literals, and const merely upgrades the inference to a readonly tuple.

facts.title: lib is ES2022 — toSorted / findLast / groupBy are errors
facts.fact: tsconfig.base.json pins lib to ["ES2022"], so ES2023+ builtins fail TS2550. Raising lib adds types only — confirm Hermes ships the method first.
facts.code:
movies.toSorted(byRating);    // TS2550 → lib es2023
movies.findLast(isTopRated);  // TS2550 → lib es2023
Object.groupBy(cast, byDept); // TS2550 → lib es2024
Object.hasOwn(m, "id");       // fine — ES2022
facts.source: Reproduced with tsc 6.0.3 at lib: ["ES2022"] (the repo's tsconfig.base.json). Object.groupBy is declared in typescript@6.0.3/lib/lib.es2024.object.d.ts and returns Partial<Record<K, T[]>> — every bucket is T[] | undefined even after a lib bump.

facts.title: ${configDir} makes a shared tsconfig's paths portable
facts.fact: In tsconfig.base.json, ${configDir} expands to the folder of the config that extends it — so paths and include can live once at the root.
facts.source: Verified with tsc 6.0.3: a pkg/tsconfig.json that only extends this base resolves @/dep to pkg/src/dep.ts and includes pkg/src/** (confirmed via --showConfig). Today apps/mobile/tsconfig.json and packages/config/tsconfig.json each redeclare paths/include.

facts.title: Valibot composes with pipe(), it doesn't chain methods
facts.fact: Validations and transforms are separate functions passed to v.pipe(). There is no .email().max() chaining — the first argument is always the schema.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2 dist/index.d.mts (declare function pipe); v.string().email is undefined

facts.title: parse throws, safeParse returns, is() only guards
facts.fact: v.parse throws a ValiError carrying .issues; v.safeParse never throws. v.is is a type guard that narrows to InferInput and applies no transforms.

facts.title: v.object silently strips unknown keys
facts.fact: v.object drops any key not in the schema. v.looseObject keeps the extras, v.strictObject rejects with a strict_object issue, and v.objectWithRest validates extras against a rest schema. Choose deliberately at an API boundary.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2: all four run against {a,b} — object -> {a}, looseObject keeps b, strictObject issues strict_object

facts.title: v.fallback wraps a schema; it is not a pipe action
facts.fact: fallback() takes a schema and returns one, so it wraps a pipe — never sits inside one. It swaps in its value whenever the pipe reports an issue.

facts.title: A fallback callback tells absent keys from invalid ones
facts.fact: In v.object a missing key calls the fallback fn with no dataset; a present-but-invalid value passes an issue-carrying dataset. Report only the latter.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2 object ~run calls getFallback(valueSchema) with no dataset arg on an absent key; verified by running both cases

facts.title: Use v.picklist for string-literal unions
facts.fact: v.picklist(["trending", "popular"]) validates a literal union in one call and infers the union type — cheaper than a v.union of v.literal. Its error names the options: 'Expected ("a" | "b") but received "c"'.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2 dist/index.d.mts line 6432 (declare function picklist); message reproduced via safeParse

facts.title: v.variant beats v.union for discriminated objects
facts.fact: On a bad tv payload, v.union reports the useless 'Invalid type: Expected Object but received Object'. v.variant("type", [...]) reads the discriminator first and reports the real issue on the real field.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2 dist/index.d.mts line 7545 (declare function variant); both messages reproduced

facts.title: One bad row shouldn't blank a list — guard per item
facts.fact: One bad element fails the whole v.array. Put v.fallback inside it, but the fallback value must fit the item type — so wrap the row in v.nullable.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2: v.safeParse(v.array(Row), [ok, bad, ok]).success === false; the nullable-fallback array yields [ok, null, ok] (v.fallback(Row, null) does not typecheck)

facts.title: safeParse's typed flag is not the same as success
facts.fact: typed:true means the value matched the schema's shape even though a validation failed, so result.output still holds it. Validations after a type failure never run: a v.check on v.string() is skipped when the input is a number.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2: safeParse(v.pipe(v.string(), v.minLength(3)), "ab") -> { typed: true, success: false, output: "ab", issues }

facts.title: v.forward moves a cross-field issue onto the right field
facts.fact: A v.check on a whole object attaches its issue to the root. Wrap it in v.forward to attach it to a specific field path instead.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2 dist/index.d.mts line 333 (declare function forward); getDotPath on the issue returns "confirm"

facts.title: InferInput and InferOutput are not the same type
facts.fact: Transforms, fallbacks and defaults make output differ from input. Type parsed data with InferOutput; type what a caller writes with InferInput.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2 dist/index.d.mts lines 3307-3311 (InferInput / InferOutput); v.parse(S, {}) -> { n: 10 }

facts.title: toJsonSchema throws on v.transform and v.brand
facts.fact: @valibot/to-json-schema throws on actions it can't represent. typeMode:"input" stops before any transform; v.brand still throws — use ignoreActions.
facts.source: @valibot/to-json-schema 1.7.1: transform and brand both throw by default; typeMode:"input" rescues transform only — brand needs ignoreActions:["brand"]

facts.title: Don't log Valibot issues — path[].input is the payload
facts.fact: issue.path[n].input is the whole parent object, sibling secrets included — and summarize() still quotes the bad value. Report v.getDotPath instead.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2, run locally: safeParse(v.object({session_id,expires}), {session_id:"SECRET",expires:"soon"}).issues[0].path[0].input === the full object; v.summarize prints 'Expected number but received "jwt-abc-123-supersecret"' untruncated; v.getDotPath(issue) -> "token"

facts.title: v.getFallbacks builds a defaults object from a schema
facts.fact: getFallback returns undefined for an object; getFallbacks walks it recursively and returns every v.fallback value. Keys with no fallback are omitted.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2 dist/index.d.mts:484 (getFallbacks -> InferFallbacks); ran both: getFallback(Home) === undefined, getFallbacks(Home) === {_schemaVersion:1, order:[]} with a no-fallback key omitted

facts.title: safeParse collects every issue unless you abortEarly
facts.fact: 3 bad fields give 3 issues. A Config third arg changes that: abortEarly stops at the first issue, abortPipeEarly at the first per pipe.
facts.code:
v.safeParse(S, bad).issues.length; // 3
v.safeParse(S, bad, { abortEarly: true })
  .issues.length; // 1

const Fast = v.config(S, { abortEarly: true });
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2 dist/index.d.mts:3705 (interface Config: lang/message/abortEarly/abortPipeEarly); reproduced 3 vs 1 issues on a 3-field object, and 2 vs 1 on a 2-action pipe with abortPipeEarly

facts.title: v.parseJson() validates a stored JSON string in one pipe
facts.fact: Pipe a stored string straight into a schema: bad JSON becomes a parse_json issue, not a thrown SyntaxError. Marked @beta in 1.4.2.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2 dist/index.d.mts:13531 (declare function parseJson, @beta); safeParse(Stored, "{not json") -> issues[0].type === "parse_json", no throw. v.stringifyJson() covers the write side.

facts.title: optional accepts missing and undefined; the others don't
facts.fact: optional accepts a missing key and an explicit undefined. exactOptional rejects the explicit undefined. undefinedable rejects a missing key.
facts.source: valibot 1.4.2: ran all three against {} and { q: undefined } — optional passes both; exactOptional fails on explicit undefined; undefinedable fails on the missing key

facts.title: v5 has no default export; TS needs the curried create
facts.fact: v5 dropped the default export and requires React 18+. In TypeScript use the curried create<T>()(...) — the empty parens are required for inference.

facts.title: Calling the store hook bare subscribes to every field
facts.fact: useStore() with no selector returns the whole state object, so a change to any field re-renders the component. Pass a selector, one call per value: const ready = useUI((s) => s.ready).

facts.title: A selector returning a new object can loop forever
facts.fact: Selector output is compared with Object.is, so a fresh object or array each render can loop forever. Wrap multi-value picks in useShallow.

facts.title: set() merges one level deep, not recursively
facts.fact: set() shallow-merges at the top level only — a nested object is replaced wholesale, so spread it yourself (or add the immer middleware).

facts.title: The hook IS the store: getState/setState outside React
facts.fact: create() copies the store API onto the hook, so getState, setState, subscribe and getInitialState work from any module — no hooks, no Provider.

facts.title: Selecting an action never causes a re-render
facts.fact: The initializer runs once, so action functions in the store are reference-stable forever: useStore((s) => s.addFavorite) never re-renders on state change. Only multi-value picks need useShallow; a single function pick does not.

facts.title: persist defaults to localStorage — dead in React Native
facts.fact: The persist default storage is localStorage, which does not exist in RN. Always pass createJSONStorage over AsyncStorage (or an MMKV adapter).

facts.title: persist over AsyncStorage hydrates after first render
facts.fact: With async storage the first render shows bundled defaults, then the store rehydrates. Gate boot UI on useStore.persist.hasHydrated(), or subscribe via useStore.persist.onFinishHydration(fn).

facts.title: partialize picks what persists; a version bump wipes it
facts.fact: persist's partialize(state) picks which fields hit storage — keep transient UI flags out. If the stored version differs from the version option and no migrate() is supplied, the saved state is discarded and defaults win.

facts.title: Slices are factories spread over the same set/get args
facts.fact: Compose slices by spreading each factory over the same (set, get, api) args in one create call; type each with StateCreator<Store, [], [], Slice>.

facts.title: setState({}, true) is a type error in v5
facts.fact: v5 split the setState overloads: replace: true now demands a complete state object, not a Partial. To truly reset (actions included), hand back the initial state: useStore.setState(useStore.getInitialState(), true).

facts.title: subscribeWithSelector gives transient, render-free updates
facts.fact: Wrap the initializer in subscribeWithSelector and store.subscribe takes (selector, cb, { fireImmediately }) — react to a value with zero re-renders.

facts.title: persist's default merge erases keys inside nested objects
facts.fact: The default merge is {...cur, ...saved} — a stored nested object replaces the default wholesale, so a key added inside it later rehydrates undefined.
facts.source: zustand/esm/middleware.mjs (default merge, set(stateFromStorage, true); merge also runs on a fresh install with persistedState === undefined)

facts.title: Hydration errors are swallowed unless onRehydrateStorage
facts.fact: A corrupt store never throws: the JSON.parse or storage error is caught and passed to the returned callback as (undefined, error). Wire it to Sentry.

facts.title: set() bails on identical state — but persist still writes
facts.fact: Returning the identical state object skips the notify (Object.is) but not the persist write — setItem() runs after every set(). Guard with get().
facts.source: zustand/esm/vanilla.mjs (if (!Object.is(nextState, state))) + esm/middleware.mjs (persist set wrapper calls setItem() unconditionally)

facts.title: Pressable is already an a11y element — Views are not
facts.fact: Pressable defaults to accessible={true}: it collapses children into one screen-reader node and builds the label from child <Text>. View does not.
facts.code:
import { Pressable, Text } from 'react-native';

<Pressable accessibilityRole="button" onPress={go}>
  <Text>Dune</Text>
  <Text>2021</Text>
</Pressable>

facts.title: aria-hidden hides a subtree on both platforms at once
facts.fact: View maps aria-hidden to accessibilityElementsHidden (iOS) and importantForAccessibility='no-hide-descendants' (Android) — one prop, not two.

facts.title: expo-image is invisible to screen readers by default
facts.fact: In expo-image 57 `accessible` defaults to false — an alt or accessibilityLabel alone will not expose a poster to VoiceOver or TalkBack. Set accessible on meaningful images; leave it off, with no label, for decorative ones.
facts.source: expo-image/build/Image.types.d.ts (accessible?: boolean — @default false) + ios/ImageModule.swift:90 (isAccessibilityElement = accessible ?? false)

facts.title: role beats accessibilityRole, and the words differ
facts.fact: RN 0.86 prefers `role` over `accessibilityRole` when both are set, and the vocabularies differ: role has 'heading', 'img', 'slider'; accessibilityRole has 'header', 'image', 'adjustable' — and no 'slider' at all.

facts.title: getByRole only finds real accessibility elements
facts.fact: RNTL 14 gates getByRole on isAccessibilityElement: a View with a role but no `accessible` prop never matches. Pressable, Text, TextInput, Switch do.
facts.code:
import { Text, View } from 'react-native';

<View accessible accessibilityRole="button">
  <Text>Dune</Text>
</View>

facts.title: The a11y lint rule passes on a button with no label
facts.fact: has-valid-accessibility-descriptors passes if ANY of role, accessibilityRole, accessibilityLabel, accessibilityActions or accessible is present — and its autofix inserts accessibilityRole="button", leaving the control unlabeled.

facts.title: Move screen-reader focus with a ref, not a reactTag
facts.fact: sendAccessibilityEvent takes a host-instance ref (Fabric-safe); setAccessibilityFocus(reactTag) is deprecated. The target View must be `accessible`.

facts.title: Bundled defaults are the floor; remote is only an override
facts.fact: The bundled default is the floor: it must render a complete, correct app with zero network. Every remote value overrides a working default, never the sole source of a structural element; one logical key lives in exactly one layer.

facts.title: Import the boot floor from a schema-free module
facts.fact: A try/catch can't catch a module-load throw, and a boot crash defeats OTA (the updater never runs). Keep the floor a plain literal, not schema output.

facts.title: Put v.fallback on each field, not around the document
facts.fact: A fallback around the whole object blanks every key when one value is bad; per-field fallbacks keep the rest. Pass a callback, not a bare undefined.

facts.title: _schemaVersion is the one field with no fallback
facts.fact: Give the version literal no fallback — a wrong version must reject the whole document. issue.path[0].key separates that from a malformed payload.

facts.title: Buffer field degradations until the document parses
facts.fact: Buffer per-field fallback reports and emit them only if the whole document parses. If the payload is rejected wholesale (bad version), those warnings describe a config nobody ever ran — send one document report instead.

facts.title: A typo'd config key is a silent no-op at runtime
facts.fact: v.object strips unknown keys, so a misspelled homeFeedTitel publishes fine and does nothing. Emit the JSON Schema with additionalProperties: false and point the config file's $schema at it — the typo errors in the editor.

facts.title: OTA ships code; remote config ships values
facts.fact: EAS Update is a deployment channel for code — the incident/safety plane. Remote config is a control plane for values — the routine operator plane. Shipping a routine value change via OTA is an anti-pattern.

facts.title: Generate the JSON Schema, then guard it against drift
facts.fact: Emit the JSON Schema from the Valibot schema, then diff it in a test: a stale artifact fails CI. Gate regen on an exact sentinel, not a truthy env.
